1.12: Daphca , perhaps Mafka, in the Maghara wadi.
1.24: A Arada , probably the Djebel Haradeh.
1.27: This holocaust is independent of the one Moses speaks of in Leviticus (see Leviticus ,
1.34: Hebrona must have been near Asiongaber.
1.47: In the mountains of Abarim , in the land of Moab, east of the Dead Sea.
1.5: Two thousand cubits , approximately one thousand meters.
1.15: In the Sinai desert , around Mount Sinai itself.
1.35: Asiongaber , seaport, at the northern end of the Elanitic Gulf.
1.22: Assur , Assyria, a kingdom whose capital was Nineveh on the Tigris.
1.11: The Sea of Cenereth is the Sea of Galilee.
1.36: It's Cadès . We have observed several similar formulas; their purpose is to express a name that has fallen into disuse, replaced by the one used at the time of the sacred writer. Cadès . See Numbers ,
1.42: Chanath, Nobé , probably the present-day Kanawa, in the southern Hauran, a few hours north of Mount el-Klub.
1.9: . ― Sedaba , today Sadad, thirty miles east of the entrance to’ Hamath .
1.40: The King of Arad . See Numbers ,

2.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
2.2: «"The children of Israel shall camp each by his banner, under the standards of his families, they shall camp opposite the tent of meeting, all around.
2.3: At the front, towards the east, will encamp the banner of the camp of Judah, with its troops; the prince of the sons of Judah is Nahshon, son of Aminadab.,
2.4: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is seventy-four thousand six hundred men.
2.5: Alongside him will camp the tribe of Issachar; the prince of the sons of Issachar is Nathanael, son of Shuar.,
2.6: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is fifty-four thousand four hundred men.
2.7: Then the tribe of Zebulun, the leader of the sons of Zebulun was Eliab, son of Helon,
2.8: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is fifty-seven thousand four hundred men.
2.9: The total number of men in the camp of Judah, according to the census, was one hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred, according to their troops. They will be the first to set out.
2.10: In the south, the banner of Reuben's camp, with his troops, the prince of Reuben's sons is Elisur, son of Zedeur,
2.11: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is forty-six thousand five hundred men.
2.12: Alongside him will encamp the tribe of Simeon; the prince of the sons of Simeon is Salamiel, son of Surisaddai.,
2.13: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is fifty-nine thousand three hundred men.
2.14: Then the tribe of Gad, the leader of the sons of Gad being Eliasaph, son of Duel,
2.15: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty men.
2.16: The total for Ruben's camp, according to the counted men, was one hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty men, according to their troops. They will be the second to set out.
2.17: Next will come the meeting tent, the Levite camp in the middle of the other camps. They will march in the order of their encampment, each in their rank, according to their banner.
2.18: To the west, the banner of Ephraim, with its troops, the prince of the sons of Ephraim is Elisama, son of Ammiud,
2.19: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is forty thousand five hundred men.
2.20: Alongside him will camp the tribe of Manasseh; the prince of the sons of Manasseh is Gamaliel, son of Phaedassur.,
2.21: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is thirty-two thousand two hundred men.
2.22: Then the tribe of Benjamin, the leader of the sons of Benjamin, is Abidan, son of Gideon,
2.23: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is thirty-five thousand four hundred men.
2.24: The total number of men in the Ephraim camp, according to the census, was one hundred and eight thousand and one hundred, according to their troops. They will be the third to set out.
2.25: To the north, the banner of the camp of Dan, with his troops, the prince of the sons of Dan is Ahiezer, son of Ammisaddai,
2.26: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is sixty-two thousand seven hundred men.
2.27: Alongside him will camp the tribe of Asher; the prince of the sons of Asher is Phegiel, son of Ochran.,
2.28: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is forty-one thousand five hundred men.
2.29: Then the tribe of Naphtali, the leader of the sons of Naphtali is Ahira, son of Enan,
2.30: and his army corps, according to the men counted, is fifty-three thousand four hundred men.
2.31: The total for Dan's camp, according to the counted men, is one hundred fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They will be the last to march, according to their banners.»
2.32: These were the children of Israel registered in the census according to their families. Total for all the men registered, divided into various camps, according to their army units: six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty men.
2.33: The Levites were not included in the census with the children of Israel, according to what the Lord had commanded Moses.
2.34: And the children of Israel did according to all that the Lord had commanded Moses. Thus they encamped, according to their banners, and thus they set out, each according to his family, according to his ancestral house.

3.1: This is the account of the descendants of Aaron and Moses, in the days when the Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai.
3.2: These are the names of Aaron's sons: Nadab, the firstborn, Abiu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
3.3: These are the names of the sons of Aaron, anointed priests, installed to exercise the priesthood.
3.4: Nadab and Abiu died before the Lord when they offered strange fire before the Lord in the desert of Sinai; they had no sons. Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests in the presence of Aaron their father.
3.5: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
3.6: «"Bring the tribe of Levi near and place them before Aaron the priest, so that they may serve him.
3.7: They shall be in charge of everything that is entrusted to his care and to the care of the whole assembly, before the tent of meeting, thus performing the service of the tabernacle.
3.8: They will be in charge of all the utensils of the tent of meeting and of what is assigned to the children of Israel: they will thus perform the service of the tabernacle.
3.9: You shall give the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they shall be entirely given to him from among the children of Israel.
3.10: You shall appoint Aaron and his sons to perform their priestly duties, and any foreigner who approaches the sanctuary shall be put to death.»
3.11: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
3.12: «Behold, I have taken the Levites from among the children of Israel in place of every firstborn among the children of Israel who opens his mother’s womb; and the Levites are mine.
3.13: For every firstborn son is mine. On the day I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated to myself every firstborn son in Israel, both of man and of beast: they are mine. I am the Lord.»
3.14: The Lord spoke to Moses in the desert of Sinai, saying:
3.15: «Take a census of the Levites by their families, by their families. You shall take a census of all the males, from one month old and upward.»
3.16: Moses took a census of them at the Lord's command, as he had been instructed.
3.17: These are the sons of Levi, according to their names: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
3.18: These are the names of Gershon's sons, according to their families: Lebni and Semei.
3.19: Sons of Kohath, according to their families: Amram, Jezaar, Hebron and Oziel.
3.20: The sons of Merari, according to their families: Moholi and Musi. These are the families of Levi according to their families.
3.21: From Gerson came the family of Lebni and the family of Shemei; these are the families of the Gersonites.
3.22: Their census, counting all males from the age of one month and above, their census was seven thousand five hundred.
3.23: The Gersonite families camped behind the tabernacle, to the west.
3.24: The prince of the patriarchal house of the Gersonites was Eliasaph, son of Lael.
3.25: As for the tent of meeting, the sons of Gershon were in charge of the tabernacle and the tent, its covering, and the curtain that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.,
3.26: the hangings of the courtyard and the curtain of the entrance to the courtyard, all around the tabernacle and the altar and its cords for all its service.
3.27: From Caath came the family of the Amramites, the family of the Jezaarites, the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites; these are the families of the Caathites.
3.28: Counting all the males from the age of one month and above, eight thousand six hundred were found, charged with guarding the sanctuary.
3.29: The families of the sons of Caath were encamped on the south side of the tabernacle.
3.30: The prince of the patriarchal house of the Caathite families was Elisaphan, son of Oziel.
3.31: The ark, the table of the Presence, the lampstand, the altars, the utensils of the sanctuary with which the service is performed, the veil, and everything related to its service were entrusted to their care.
3.32: The chief of the Levites was Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest; he was in charge of those who were responsible for guarding the sanctuary.
3.33: From Merari come the family of the Moholites and the family of the Musites: these are the families of the Merarites.
3.34: Their census, counting all males from the age of one month and above, was six thousand two hundred.
3.35: The prince of the patriarchal house of the families of Merari was Suriel, son of Abihaiel. They were encamped on the north side of the tabernacle.
3.36: The sons of Merari were responsible for the care and safekeeping of the tabernacle's boards, its crossbeams, its columns and their bases, all its utensils and all its services,
3.37: columns of the forecourt all around, their bases, their piles and their ropes.
3.38: Opposite the tabernacle, to the east, before the tent of meeting, to the east, Moses, Aaron and his sons were encamped, they were in charge of the sanctuary, for what was entrusted to the care of the children of Israel, the stranger who approached it was to be punished with death.
3.39: The total number of Levites counted, whom Moses counted according to the Lord’s command, of all males from one month old and above, was twenty-two thousand.
3.40: The Lord said to Moses, «Take a census of all the firstborn males among the children of Israel, from one month old and above, and count their names.
3.41: »You shall take the Levites for me, I am the Lord, in place of all the firstborn of the children of Israel, and the livestock of the Levites in place of all the firstborn of the livestock of the children of Israel.”
3.42: Moses took a census of all the firstborn among the children of Israel, according to the order that the Lord had given him.
3.43: All the firstborn males, counted by name, from the age of one month and above, of whom a census was taken, were twenty-two thousand two hundred and seventy-three.
3.44: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
3.45: «Take the Levites in place of all the firstborn of the children of Israel, and the livestock of the Levites in place of their livestock, and the Levites shall be mine. I am the Lord.”.
3.46: For the redemption of the two hundred and seventy-three firstborn of the children of Israel who exceed the number of Levites,
3.47: You shall take five shekels per head, according to the sanctuary shekel, which is twenty gerahs.
3.48: You shall give the money to Aaron and his sons for the redemption of those who exceed the number of Levites.»
3.49: Moses took the money for the redemption of those who exceeded the number of firstborn redeemed by the Levites,
3.50: He took the money from the firstborn of the children of Israel, one thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
3.51: And Moses gave the ransom money to Aaron and his sons, at the Lord's command, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

4.1: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
4.2: «Count the sons of Kohath among the children of Levi, by clans and families,
4.3: From the age of thirty and above up to the age of fifty, all those who have to do service, to perform some function in the meeting tent.
4.4: This is what the service of the sons of Kohath will be in the tent of meeting: it will be concerning the most holy things.
4.5: When the camp is broken up, Aaron and his sons will come and draw down the veil and cover the ark of the testimony with it.,
4.6: They will put a covering of dolphin skins over it and spread over it a whole sheet of purple cloth, then they will put the poles of the ark in place.
4.7: They shall spread a purple cloth over the table of the showbread and place on it the dishes, the cups, the bowls, and the drinking vessels; the perpetual bread shall be on it.,
4.8: They will spread over it a crimson cloth, which they will wrap with a covering of dolphin skins, and they will place the table bars.
4.9: They shall take a purple cloth and cover the lampstand, its lamps, its tongs, its ash jugs, and all its oil vessels, necessary for its service,
4.10: Then, having placed him, with all his utensils, in a covering of dolphin skins, they will place him on the stretcher.
4.11: They will spread a purple cloth over the golden altar and, after wrapping it with a covering of dolphin skins, they will put the poles on it.
4.12: They shall take all the utensils used for service in the sanctuary and, having put them in a purple cloth, they shall wrap them in a covering of dolphin skins and place them on the stretcher.
4.13: They shall remove the ashes from the altar and spread a scarlet cloth over them,
4.14: They will place on it all the utensils necessary for its service, the braziers, the forks, the shovels, the basins, all the utensils of the altar and, having spread over everything a covering of dolphin skins, they will put the bars on it.
4.15: When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all its furnishings, and when the camp is broken up, the Kohathites shall come and carry them off, but they shall not touch the holy things, lest they die. This is what the Kohathites shall carry into the tent of meeting.
4.16: Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, shall be in charge of the oil for the lampstand, the fragrant incense, the regular grain offering, and the anointing oil; he shall be in charge of the entire tabernacle and all that is in it, the sanctuary and all its furnishings.»
4.17: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
4.18: «Be careful not to cut off the tribe of the families of the Kaathites from among the Levites.
4.19: Treat them in this way, so that they may live and not die when they approach the most holy things. Aaron and his sons will come and assign to each of them his service and what he is to carry,
4.20: and the Levites shall not enter to see the holy things for even a moment, lest they die.»
4.21: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
4.22: «"Also count Gerson's sons according to their families, according to their families,
4.23: You shall take a census of them, from the age of thirty and above up to the age of fifty, of all those who have to do service, to perform any function in the tent of meeting.
4.24: Here is the service for the families of the Gersonites, what they will have to do and carry.
4.25: They shall carry the hangings of the tabernacle and the tent of meeting, its covering and the covering of dolphin skins that goes over it, the curtain that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
4.26: the hangings of the courtyard and the curtain of the entrance of the gate of the courtyard, all around the tabernacle and the altar, their cords and all the utensils for their use, and they shall perform all the service which relates to them.
4.27: All the service of the sons of the Gershonites shall be under the command of Aaron and his sons, for all that they shall have to carry and for all that they shall have to do, you shall entrust to their care all the things that they have to carry.
4.28: This is the service of the families of the sons of the Gershonites with regard to the tent of meeting; they shall perform their duty under the direction of Ithamar, son of Aaron, the priest.
4.29: You shall take a census of the sons of Merari by clans and families,
4.30: You shall take a census of them, from the age of thirty and above up to the age of fifty, all those who have to do service, to perform some function in the tent of meeting.
4.31: This is what will be entrusted to them, what they will have to carry according to all their service in the tent of meeting: the boards of the tabernacle, its crossbars, its pillars, its bases,
4.32: The columns of the courtyard surrounding it, their bases, their stakes, their ropes, all their utensils and everything related to their service. You shall make an inventory by name of the objects entrusted to them to carry.
4.33: This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari, all their service with regard to the tent of meeting, under the direction of Ithamar, son of Aaron, the priest.
4.34: Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of the assembly took a census of the sons of the Kohathites according to their clans and families,
4.35: From the age of thirty and above up to the age of fifty, all those who had to do service, to perform some function in the meeting tent.
4.36: The number of those counted, according to their families, was two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
4.37: These were the censuses of the families of the Kaathites, all those who performed a service in the tent of meeting. Moses and Aaron took the census according to the Lord's command through Moses.
4.38: The census of Gerson's sons according to their families and according to their families,
4.39: From the age of thirty and above up to the age of fifty, all those who had to perform service, to fulfill some function in the assembly tent,
4.40: The number counted by clans and families was two thousand six hundred and thirty.
4.41: These were the registered members of the families of Gershon's sons, all those who performed a service in the tent of meeting. Moses and Aaron took a census of them at the Lord's command.
4.42: The censuses of the families of the sons of Merari, categorized by clan and family,
4.43: From the age of thirty and above up to the age of fifty, all those who had to perform service, to fulfill some function in the assembly tent,
4.44: The number of people counted, according to their families, was three thousand two hundred.
4.45: These were the censuses of the families of the sons of Merari. Moses and Aaron took a census of them at the Lord's command through Moses.
4.46: All the Levites counted, including Moses, Aaron, and the princes of Israel, according to their families and clans,
4.47: From the age of thirty and above up to the age of fifty, all those who had to perform any function, in service and transport, with regard to the assembly tent,
4.48: The total number of those counted was eight thousand five hundred and eighty.
4.49: They were counted according to the Lord's command through Moses, assigning to each one the service he was to do and what he was to carry; thus they were counted, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

5.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
5.2: «"Command the children of Israel to expel from the camp anyone who has leprosy and anyone who has gonorrhea and anyone who is defiled by a dead body.
5.3: "Men and women, you must bring them out of the camp so they do not defile their camp, in the midst of which I live."»
5.4: The children of Israel did so and they brought them out of the camp, as the Lord had commanded Moses, so did the children of Israel.
5.5: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
5.6: «Say to the children of Israel: If any man or woman commits any of the sins that harms their neighbor, by being unfaithful to the Lord and having become guilty,
5.7: He shall confess his sin and he shall return in full the ill-gotten object, adding a fifth to it, and he shall give it to the one against whom he has been guilty.
5.8: If the guilty party has no representative to whom the object of the offense can be returned, this object reverts to the Lord, to the priest, in addition to the expiatory ram with which atonement will be made for the guilty party.
5.9: «Every offering taken from the holy things that the children of Israel present to the priest shall belong to him,
5.10: "The things that each man has consecrated will belong to him, and what each one gives to the priest will belong to him.".
5.11: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
5.12: «Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: If a married woman turns away and becomes unfaithful to her husband,
5.13: another man having had relations with her and that the matter was hidden from the eyes of her husband, this woman having defiled herself in secret, without there being any witness against her and without her being caught in the act:
5.14: If the husband is overcome by a spirit of jealousy and is jealous of his wife who has defiled herself, or if he is overcome by a spirit of jealousy and is jealous even though his wife has not defiled herself:
5.15: This man shall bring his wife to the priest and bring an offering for her, a tenth of an ephah of barley flour; he shall not pour oil on it nor put incense on it, for it is an offering of jealousy, an offering of remembrance, which recalls a transgression.
5.16: The priest will have her approach the altar and stand before the Lord.
5.17: The priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel and, having taken some dust from the floor of the tabernacle, shall put it into the water.
5.18: The priest will have the woman stand before the Lord, he will untie the hair from her head, and place the memorial offering, the offering of jealousy, in her hands. The priest will also have the bitter waters in his hand, which bring the curse.
5.19: The priest will adjure the woman and say to her: If no man has lain with you and if you have not turned away to defile yourself, being under the power of your husband, be preserved from the effect of these bitter waters which bring the curse.
5.20: But if, while under the power of your husband, you have turned away and defiled yourself, and if another man besides your husband has lain with you:
5.21: The priest shall adjure the woman by the oath of imprecation and say to her: May the Lord make you a curse and an accursion among your people, making your waist thin and your belly swell,
5.22: And may these waters that bring the curse enter your bowels, causing your belly to swell and your sides to become thin. And the woman will say: Amen. Amen.
5.23: The priest will write these imprecations on a scroll and then erase them in the bitter waters.
5.24: Then he will make the woman drink the bitter waters that bring the curse, and the waters that bring the curse will enter her and be bitter to her.
5.25: The priest, having taken the offering of jealousy from the woman's hands, will wave it before the Lord and bring it near the altar,
5.26: He will take a handful of this offering as a memorial and will burn it on the altar, and after that, he will give the woman the water to drink.
5.27: When he makes her drink the waters, it will happen, if she has defiled herself and been unfaithful to her husband, that the waters which bring the curse will enter her and be bitter: her belly will swell, her sides will become thin, and that woman will be a curse in the midst of her people.
5.28: But if the woman has not defiled herself and is pure, she will be preserved and will have children.
5.29: This is the law concerning jealousy, when a woman, being under the power of her husband, turns away and defiles herself,
5.30: or when a husband is overcome by jealousy and becomes jealous of his wife: he shall have his wife stand before the Lord and the priest shall apply to him this entire law.
5.31: The husband will be free from sin, but the wife will bear her iniquity.

6.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
6.2: «Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: When a man or a woman makes a vow, a Nazirite vow, to separate in honor of the Lord,
6.3: He shall abstain from wine and intoxicating drinks, he shall not drink vinegar made with wine, nor vinegar made with intoxicating drinks, he shall not drink any grape juice, he shall not eat fresh grapes, nor raisins.
6.4: Throughout his Nazirite vows, he will eat nothing from the grapevine, from the seeds to the skin of the grape.
6.5: During the entire period of his Nazirite vow, no razor shall be used on his head, until the completion of the days for which he sets himself apart in honor of the Lord, he shall be holy, letting his hair grow freely.
6.6: During the entire time he separates himself in honor of the Lord, he shall not approach any dead body,
6.7: He shall not defile himself for his father, nor for his mother, nor for his brother or sister, when they die, for he bears on his head the consecration to his God.
6.8: Throughout his time as a Nazirite, he was devoted to the Lord.
6.9: If anyone dies suddenly near him and his consecrated head is thus defiled, he shall shave his head on the day of his purification, he shall shave it on the seventh day.
6.10: And on the eighth day, he shall bring to the priest two turtledoves or two young pigeons, to the entrance of the tent of meeting
6.11: The priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, and he shall make atonement for him for his sin at the time of his death. On that day the Nazirite shall consecrate his head.
6.12: He will again consecrate to the Lord the days of his Nazirite vow and he will offer a year-old lamb as a guilt offering; the previous days are null and void, because his Nazirite vow was defiled.
6.13: This is the law of the Nazarene. On the day he has completed the time of his Nazarene service, he shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting.
6.14: He shall present his offering to the Lord: a lamb a year old, without blemish, for a burnt offering, a ewe a year old, without blemish, for a sin offering, a ram without blemish for a peace offering,
6.15: as well as a basket of unleavened bread, cakes of fine flour kneaded with oil and unleavened wafers sprinkled with oil, with the oblation and the usual libations.
6.16: The priest will present them before the Lord and offer his sin offering and his burnt offering.
6.17: Then he shall offer the ram as a peace offering to the Lord, with the basket of unleavened bread, the priest shall make his offering and his drink offering.
6.18: The Nazarene shall shave his consecrated head at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and taking the hair of his consecrated head, he shall put it on the fire which is under the victim of the peace offering.
6.19: The priest shall take the shoulder of the ram when it is cooked, an unleavened cake from the basket, and an unleavened wafer, and, having placed them on the hands of the Nazarene, after he has shaved his consecrated head,
6.20: The priest shall wave them before the Lord: they are holy things belonging to the priest, besides the waved breast and the cut-off thigh. After this, the Nazirite may drink wine.
6.21: This is the law of the Nazirite who has taken a vow; this is his offering to the Lord for his Nazirite vow, beyond what his means allow him to do. According to the vow he has made, so he shall act according to the law of his Nazirite vow.»
6.22: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
6.23: «Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying: ‘This is how you shall bless the children of Israel, saying to them:’
6.24: May the Lord bless you and keep you.
6.25: May the Lord make his face shine upon you and grant you his grace.
6.26: May the Lord turn his face toward you and give you peace.
6.27: "That is how they will put my name on the children of Israel, and I will bless them."»

7.1: On the day Moses finished setting up the tabernacle, anointing and consecrating it with all its furnishings, and the altar with all its furnishings, when he had anointed and consecrated them,
7.2: The princes of Israel, heads of their families, presented their offerings: these were the princes of the tribes, those who had presided over the census.
7.3: They brought their offering before the Lord: six covered chariots and twelve oxen, one chariot for two princes and one ox for each prince, and they presented them before the tabernacle.
7.4: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
7.5: «Receive these things from them and let them be used for the service of the tent of meeting; you shall give them to the Levites, to each according to the needs of his service.»
7.6: Moses, having taken the chariots and the oxen, handed them over to the Levites.
7.7: He gave two chariots and four oxen to Gerson's sons, according to the needs of their service,
7.8: He gave four chariots and eight oxen to the sons of Merari, according to the needs of their service, under the supervision of Ithamar, son of Aaron, the priest.
7.9: But he did not give any to the sons of Caath, because, having the service of the sacred objects, they had to carry them on their shoulders.
7.10: The princes presented their offering for the dedication of the altar; on the day it was anointed, the princes presented their offering before the altar.
7.11: And the Lord said to Moses, «Let a prince come each day to present his offering for the dedication of the altar.»
7.12: The one who presented his offering on the first day was Nahshon, son of Aminadab, of the tribe of Judah.
7.13: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels, a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.14: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.15: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.16: a goat for the sin offering,
7.17: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nahashon, son of Aminadab.
7.18: On the second day, Nathanael, son of Suar, prince of the tribe of Issachar, presented his offering.
7.19: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels, a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.20: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.21: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.22: a goat for the sin offering,
7.23: and, for the peaceful sacrifice, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nathanael, son of Shuar.
7.24: On the third day came Eliab, son of Helon, the prince of the sons of Zebulun,
7.25: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.26: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.27: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.28: a goat for the sin offering,
7.29: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliab, son of Helon.
7.30: On the fourth day came Elizur, son of Zedeur, the prince of the Reubenites,
7.31: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.32: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.33: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.34: a goat for the sin offering,
7.35: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elizur, son of Zedeur.
7.36: On the fifth day came Salamiel, the son of Surisaddai, the prince of the sons of Simeon.,
7.37: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.38: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.39: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.40: a goat for the sin offering,
7.41: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Salamiel, son of Surisaddai.
7.42: On the sixth day came Eliasaph son of Duel, the prince of the Gadites,
7.43: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.44: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.45: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.46: a goat for the sin offering,
7.47: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliasaph son of Duel.
7.48: On the seventh day came Elishama son of Ammiud, the prince of the Ephraimites,
7.49: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.50: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.51: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.52: a goat for the sin offering,
7.53: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elishama, son of Ammiud.
7.54: On the eighth day came Gamaliel, son of Phadassur, the prince of the sons of Manasseh,
7.55: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.56: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.57: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.58: a goat for the sin offering,
7.59: and, for the peaceful sacrifice, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Gamaliel, son of Phadassur.
7.60: On the ninth day came Abidan, son of Gideon, the prince of the Benjaminites.
7.61: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering.
7.62: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume
7.63: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.64: a goat for the sin offering
7.65: and for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Abidan, son of Gideon.
7.66: On the tenth day came Ahiezer son of Ammisaddai, the prince of the Danites.
7.67: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.68: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.69: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.70: a goat for the sin offering
7.71: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five one-year-old lambs. This was the offering of Ahiezer, son of Ammisaddai.
7.72: On the eleventh day came Pegiel son of Ochran, the prince of the Asherites.
7.73: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering.
7.74: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume,
7.75: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.76: a goat for the sin offering,
7.77: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Phegiel, son of Ochran.
7.78: On the twelfth day came Ahira, son of Enan, the prince of the sons of Naphtali.
7.79: He offered: a silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and a silver bowl weighing seventy shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with the finest flour mixed with oil, for the offering,
7.80: a golden cup of ten shekels, full of perfume
7.81: a young bull, a ram, and a year-old lamb for the burnt offering,
7.82: a goat for the sin offering
7.83: and, for the peace offering, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five lambs a year old. This was the offering of Ahira, son of Enan.
7.84: These were the gifts of the princes of Israel for the dedication of the altar on the day it was anointed: twelve silver platters, twelve silver bowls, and twelve gold cups,
7.85: Each silver dish weighed one hundred and thirty shekels and each cup seventy, the total value of the silver in these utensils being two thousand four hundred shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel,
7.86: the twelve gold cups full of perfume, each of ten shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel, total of the gold of the cups: one hundred and twenty shekels.
7.87: Total number of animals for the burnt offering: twelve young bulls, twelve rams and twelve one-year-old lambs, with their offerings. Twelve male goats for the sin offering.
7.88: The total number of animals for the peaceful sacrifice was twenty-four oxen, sixty rams, sixty male goats, and sixty one-year-old lambs. These were the gifts offered for the dedication of the altar, after it had been anointed.
7.89: When Moses entered the tent of meeting to speak with the Lord, he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the testimony, between the two cherubim. And he spoke to him.

8.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
8.2: «Speak to Aaron and tell him: When you place the lamps on the lampstand, the seven lamps will give their light on the front of the lampstand.»
8.3: Aaron did so, placing the lamps in front of the lampstand, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
8.4: The lampstand was made of hammered gold, from its base to its flowers, it was made of hammered gold; Moses had made it according to the pattern that the Lord had shown him.
8.5: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
8.6: «Take the Levites from among the children of Israel and purify them.
8.7: This is how you shall purify them: Sprinkle them with the water of purification, have them shave their entire bodies, wash their clothes, and thus purify themselves.
8.8: They shall then take a young bull for the burnt offering, with its offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and you shall take a second young bull for the sin offering.
8.9: You shall bring the Levites near to the tent of meeting and you shall summon the whole assembly of the children of Israel.
8.10: You shall bring the Levites near before the Lord, and the children of Israel shall lay their hands on the Levites.
8.11: Aaron shall offer the Levites as a wave offering before the Lord, on behalf of the children of Israel, so that they may serve the Lord.
8.12: The Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the bulls, and you shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to the Lord, to make atonement for the Levites.
8.13: You shall make the Levites stand before Aaron and before his sons and you shall offer them as a wave offering to the Lord.
8.14: You shall separate the Levites from among the children of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine.,
8.15: Afterwards, the Levites will come to perform the service in the tent of meeting. This is how you will purify them and offer them as a wave offering.
8.16: For they are entirely given to me from among the children of Israel; I have taken them for myself instead of every firstborn son, who opened his mother's womb, every firstborn son of the children of Israel.
8.17: For every firstborn among the children of Israel is mine, both of men and of animals, on the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated them to myself.
8.18: And I took the Levites in place of all the firstborn of the children of Israel,
8.19: And I gave all the Levites to Aaron and his sons from among the children of Israel, to perform the service of the children of Israel in the tent of meeting, to make atonement for the children of Israel, so that the children of Israel would not be struck by any plague when they approached the sanctuary.»
8.20: Moses, Aaron, and the whole assembly of the children of Israel did to the Levites all that the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Levites; so the children of Israel did to them.
8.21: The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes, Aaron offered them as a wave offering before the Lord and made atonement for them, in order to purify them.
8.22: After this, the Levites came to perform their service in the tent of meeting, in the presence of Aaron and his sons. As the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so it was done to them.
8.23: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
8.24: «This is what concerns the Levites. From twenty-five years old and above, the Levite shall enter into the service of the tent of meeting to perform a function.
8.25: From the age of fifty, he will leave office and will no longer serve.,
8.26: He will help his brothers in the tent of meeting to keep what is required, but he will no longer perform any other service. You shall do the same with the Levites concerning their duties.»

9.1: The Lord spoke to Moses in the desert of Sinai in the first month of the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt. He said:
9.2: «"Let the children of Israel celebrate the Passover at the appointed time.".
9.3: You shall perform it at the appointed time, on the fourteenth day of this month, between the two evenings: this is the appointed time, you shall perform it according to all the laws and ordinances that pertain to it.»
9.4: So Moses spoke to the children of Israel, so that they might celebrate the Passover.
9.5: And they celebrated the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month, at twilight, in the desert of Sinai. According to all that the Lord had commanded Moses, so did the children of Israel.
9.6: There were some men who were unclean because of a dead body and could not celebrate Passover that day. They presented themselves that same day before Moses and Aaron,
9.7: They said to Moses, «We are unclean because of a dead body; why should we be deprived of presenting the Lord’s offering at the appointed time among the Israelites?»
9.8: Moses answered them, "Wait till I hear what the Lord commands you."«
9.9: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
9.10: «Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: If anyone among you or among your descendants is found unclean because of a dead body or is on a journey far away, he shall keep the Passover to the Lord.
9.11: They shall make it in the second month, on the fourteenth day, between the two evenings, and they shall eat it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
9.12: They shall leave none of it until morning, nor shall they break its bones. They shall celebrate it according to all the statutes relating to the Passover.
9.13: If anyone, being pure and not on a journey, fails to observe the Passover, he shall be cut off from his people, because he did not present the offering of the Lord at the appointed time; he shall bear his sin.
9.14: If a foreigner residing among you celebrates the Passover of the Lord, he must observe the laws and ordinances concerning the Passover. The same law applies to you, to the foreigner as well as to the native-born Israelite.»
9.15: On the day the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, which is the tent of testimony. From evening until morning there was over the tabernacle something like the appearance of fire.
9.16: This continued: the cloud covered the tabernacle, and at night it had the appearance of fire.
9.17: When the cloud lifted from above the tent, the children of Israel broke camp and, where the cloud stopped, they pitched their camp.
9.18: At the Lord’s command, the children of Israel broke camp, and at the Lord’s command they pitched it; they remained encamped as long as the cloud rested over the tabernacle.
9.19: When the cloud remained for a long time over the tabernacle, the children of Israel observed the commandment of the Lord and did not break camp.
9.20: It was the same when the cloud only stayed a few days over the tabernacle: at the Lord's command they pitched camp and at the Lord's command they broke it up.
9.21: If the cloud rested only from evening to morning and rose in the morning, they broke camp, or, if the cloud rose after a day and a night, they broke camp.
9.22: If the cloud remained over the tabernacle for several days, a month, or a year, the children of Israel remained encamped and did not break camp, but as soon as it lifted, they broke camp.
9.23: At the Lord's command they pitched camp and at the Lord's command they broke camp; they observed the Lord's command, according to the Lord's order given by Moses.

10.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
10.2: «Make yourself two silver trumpets, you shall make them of hammered silver. They shall be used for summoning the assembly and for setting out the camps.
10.3: When the bell rings, the whole assembly will gather around you at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
10.4: If only one trumpet is sounded, only the princes, the leaders of the thousands of Israel, will gather around you.
10.5: When you sound the loud bell, those encamped in the east will set out.,
10.6: When you sound the bell loudly for the second time, those who are camping at midday will set off, and the bell will sound loudly for their departure.
10.7: You will also ring to summon the assembly, but not loudly.
10.8: Aaron's sons, the priests, shall sound the trumpets: this shall be a perpetual statute for you and for your descendants.
10.9: When you go to war in your country against the enemy who attacks you, you will sound the trumpets loudly and you will be remembered by the Lord your God and you will be delivered from your enemies.
10.10: »On your days of joy, at your festivals, and at your new moons, you shall sound the trumpets over your burnt offerings and peace offerings, and they shall be a memorial before your God. I am the Lord your God.”
10.11: In the second year, on the twentieth day of the second month, the cloud rose from above the tabernacle of the testimony
10.12: And the children of Israel, resuming their marches, departed from the desert of Sinai, and the cloud stopped in the desert of Paran.
10.13: They set out for the first time, following the Lord's command through Moses.
10.14: The banner of the camp of the sons of Judah set out first, according to their troops, and the troop of Judah was commanded by Nahshon, son of Aminadab.,
10.15: The troop from the tribe of the sons of Issachar was commanded by Nathanael, son of Shuar,
10.16: and the troop of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun was commanded by Eliab, son of Helon.
10.17: The tabernacle was taken down and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari departed, carrying the tabernacle.
10.18: The banner of Reuben's camp departed, according to their troops, and Reuben's troop was commanded by Elisur, son of Sedeur.,
10.19: The troop of the tribe of the sons of Simeon was commanded by Salamiel, son of Surisaddai,
10.20: and the troop of the tribe of the sons of Gad was commanded by Eliasaph, son of Duel.
10.21: The Kaathites left carrying the sacred objects, and the others set up the tabernacle, awaiting their arrival.
10.22: The banner of the camp of the sons of Ephraim set out, according to their troops, and the troop of Ephraim was commanded by Elisama, son of Ammiud,
10.23: The troop from the tribe of the sons of Manasseh was commanded by Gamaliel, son of Phaedassur,
10.24: and the troop of the tribe of Benjamin was commanded by Abidan, son of Gideon.
10.25: The banner of the camp of the sons of Dan set out, according to their troops: it formed the rearguard of all the camps. The troop of Dan was commanded by Ahiezer, son of Ammisaddai,
10.26: The troop of the tribe of the sons of Asher was commanded by Phegiel, son of Ochran,
10.27: and the troop of the sons of Naphtali was commanded by Ahira, son of Enan.
10.28: This was the order of march of the children of Israel according to their troops, and they set out.
10.29: Moses said to Hobab, son of Raguel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, "We are going to the place of which the Lord said, 'I will give it to you.' Come with us, and we will treat you well, for the Lord has promised to treat Israel well."«
10.30: Hobab replied, "I will not go, but I will go back to my country and my family."«
10.31: And Moses said, «Please do not leave us, since you know the places where we will camp in the desert, you will be our eye.”.
10.32: If you come with us, we will share with you the good that the Lord will do for us.»
10.33: Having set out from the mountain of the Lord, they traveled for three days, and during these three days of travel, the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them to seek a place of rest for them.
10.34: The cloud of the Lord was over them during the day as they set out from the camp.
10.35: When the ark departed, Moses would say, «Arise, Lord, and let your enemies be scattered. Let those who hate you flee before your face.»
10.36: And when she stopped, he would say, "Return, Lord, to the myriads of the thousands of Israel."«

11.1: The people began to murmur evil in the Lord's hearing. The Lord heard it, and his anger was kindled, and the fire of the Lord was kindled against them, and it devoured at the edge of the camp.
11.2: The people cried out to Moses, and Moses prayed to the Lord, and the fire went out.
11.3: That place was called Tabeera, because the fire of the Lord was kindled against them.
11.4: The group of people who were in the midst of Israel became inflamed with desire, and even the children of Israel began to weep again and said, «Who will give us meat to eat?”
11.5: It reminds us of the fish we used to eat for free in Egypt, cucumbers, melons, leeks, onions and garlic.
11.6: Now our soul is parched, nothing remains. Our eyes see only manna.»
11.7: The manna was similar to coriander seed and had the appearance of bdellium.
11.8: The people would spread out to gather it, grind it with millstones or pound it in a mortar, cook it in a pot and make cakes from it. It tasted like an oil cake.
11.9: When the dew fell on the camp during the night, so did the manna.
11.10: Moses heard the people weeping in each family, each at the entrance of their tent. The Lord's anger burned brightly. Moses was grieved,
11.11: And he said to the Lord, «Why have you done this evil to your servant, and why have I not found favor in your eyes, that you have placed upon me the burden of all these people?”
11.12: Did I conceive all these people? Did I give birth to them, that you should say to me: Carry them in your arms, as a nurse carries a nursing child, to the land that you swore to give to their fathers?
11.13: Where shall I get meat to give to all these people? For they are weeping around me, saying, “Give us meat to eat.”.
11.14: I cannot, by myself, bear the weight of all these people; they are too heavy for me.
11.15: "If you think I'm going to treat you this way, then kill me instead, I beg you, if I have found favor in your eyes and do not want me to see my own misfortune."»
11.16: The Lord said to Moses, «Gather for me seventy men from the elders of Israel, whom you know to be elders of the people and officers over them, and bring them to the tent of meeting, and let them stand there with you.
11.17: I will come down and speak to you there, and I will take some of the spirit that is on you and put it on them, so that they may share with you the burden of the people and you will no longer bear it alone.
11.18: You shall say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow and you will have meat to eat, since you have wept in the hearing of the Lord, saying, “Who will give us meat to eat? For we were better off in Egypt.” And the Lord will give you meat and you will eat it.,
11.19: You will not eat of it for one day, nor two days, nor five days, nor ten days, nor twenty days,
11.20: but for a whole month, until it comes out of your nostrils and you loathe it, because you have rejected the Lord who is among you and have wept before him, saying, »Why did we ever come out of Egypt?”
11.21: Moses said, «The people among whom I am are six hundred thousand men on foot, and you say, ‘I will give them meat and they will eat it for a whole month.
11.22: Will sheep and cattle be slaughtered for them, so that they may have enough? Or will all the fish of the sea be gathered for them, so that they may have enough?»
11.23: The Lord answered Moses, «Is the Lord’s arm too short? You will now see whether what I have said will happen to you or not.»
11.24: Moses went out and reported the words of the Lord to the people, and he gathered seventy men of the elders of the people and placed them around the tent.
11.25: The Lord descended in the cloud and spoke to Moses, and took some of the spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy elders, and when the spirit rested on them they prophesied, but they did not continue.
11.26: Two men, one named Eldad and the other Medad, had remained in the camp and the spirit rested on them also; they were among those registered, but they had not gone to the tent and they prophesied in the camp.
11.27: A young boy ran to tell Moses, saying, "Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp."«
11.28: Immediately Joshua, son of Nun, Moses' servant from his youth, spoke up and said, "Moses, my lord, stop them."«
11.29: Moses answered him, «Are you jealous for my sake? Would that all the Lord’s people were prophets and that the Lord would put his Spirit on them.»
11.30: And Moses withdrew to the camp, he and the elders of Israel.
11.31: A wind blew by the Lord's command, which brought quail from the sea and brought them down on the camp, over an area of about a day's journey on one side, and about a day's journey on the other side, around the camp, and there were nearly two cubits high on the surface of the ground.
11.32: Throughout that day, all night and all the next day, the people got up and gathered the quails, the one who had gathered the fewest had ten gomors and they spread them out for themselves all around the camp.
11.33: But the flesh was still between their teeth, before it was eaten, when the Lord’s anger burned against the people, and the Lord struck the people with a very great plague.
11.34: This place was given the name Qibroth-Hattaava, because the people who had become inflamed with covetousness were buried there.
11.35: From Qibroth-Hattaava the people set out for Haseroth and they stopped at Haseroth.

12.1: Miriam, along with Aaron, spoke against Moses concerning the Cushite woman whom he had married, for he had married a Cushite woman.
12.2: They said, «Did the Lord speak only through Moses? Did he not also speak through us?» And the Lord heard it.
12.3: But Moses was a very meek man, more so than any other man who was on the face of the earth.
12.4: Suddenly the Lord said to Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, «Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting.» So the three of them went out,
12.5: And the Lord descended in the pillar of cloud and stood at the entrance of the tent. He called Aaron and Miriam, who both came forward,
12.6: And he said, «Listen carefully to my words: if you have any prophet of the Lord, it is in visions that I reveal myself to him, it is in dreams that I speak to him.
12.7: Such is not my servant Moses, who is known to be faithful in all my house.
12.8: I speak to him face to face, showing myself and not in riddles, and he beholds the face of the Lord. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?»
12.9: And the Lord's anger burned against them, and he went away.,
12.10: The cloud departed from above the tent. And behold, Miriam became leprous, white as snow. Aaron turned toward Miriam, and behold, she was leprous.
12.11: And Aaron said to Moses, «Please, my lord, do not place upon us this sin which we have foolishly committed and of which we are guilty.
12.12: Ah. May she not be like the stillborn child whose flesh is half-consumed as it emerges from its mother's womb.»
12.13: Moses cried out to the Lord, saying, «O God, I pray you, heal her.»
12.14: The Lord said to Moses, «If her father had spat in her face, would she not have been ashamed for seven days? Let her be kept outside the camp for seven days, and after that she may be received back in.»
12.15: So Mary was kept outside the camp for seven days and the people did not leave until Mary had been received.
12.16: After that, the people departed from Haseroth and they camped in the desert of Pharan.

13.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
13.2: «"Send men to explore the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the children of Israel. You shall send one man from each of the ancestral tribes, all of them leaders."»
13.3: Moses sent them from the desert of Paran, according to the Lord's command; all these men were leaders of the children of Israel.
13.4: These are their names: for the tribe of Reuben, Sammua, son of Zeshur,
13.5: for the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat, son of Huri,
13.6: for the tribe of Judah, Caleb, son of Jephone,
13.7: for the tribe of Issachar, Igal, son of Joseph,
13.8: for the tribe of Ephraim, Hosea, son of Nun,
13.9: for the tribe of Benjamin, Phalti, son of Raphu,
13.10: for the tribe of Zebulun, Geddiel, son of Sodi,
13.11: for the tribe of Joseph, the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi, son of Susi,
13.12: for the tribe of Dan, Ammiel, son of Gemalli,
13.13: for the tribe of Asher, Shur, son of Michael,
13.14: for the tribe of Naphtali, Nahabi, son of Vapsi,
13.15: for the tribe of Gad, Guel, son of Machi.
13.16: These are the names of the men whom Moses sent to explore the land. Moses gave Hoshea, son of Nun, the name Joshua.
13.17: Moses sent them to explore the land of Canaan, he told them: «Go up there by the Negev and you will go up into the mountain.
13.18: You will examine the country, what it is like, and the people who inhabit it, whether they are strong or weak, few in number or considerable,
13.19: what the country where he lives is like, whether it is good or bad, what the cities where he lives are like, whether they are open or fortified,
13.20: "What is the soil like, whether it is rich or poor, whether there are trees or not. Be brave and pick some local fruit." It was the time of the first grapes.
13.21: They went up and explored the land, from the desert of Sin to Rohob, on the way to Emath.
13.22: They went up into the Negev and came to Hebron, where Ahiman, Sisai, and Tolmai, the sons of Enach, were. Hebron had been built seven years before Tanis of Egypt.
13.23: Arriving at the Escol Valley, they cut a vine branch with its bunch of grapes and carried it by two people using a pole; they also took pomegranates and figs.
13.24: This place was given the name Valley of Escol, because of the bunch of grapes that the children of Israel cut there.
13.25: After forty days, they returned from exploring the country.
13.26: They went and came to Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the children of Israel, at Kadesh in the wilderness of Paran. They brought a report to them and to all the congregation and showed them the fruits of the land.
13.27: This is the account they gave to Moses: «We went to the land to which you sent us. It is truly a land flowing with milk and honey, and these are its fruits.
13.28: But the people who inhabit the country are powerful and the cities are fortified and very large; we even saw children of Enac there.
13.29: The Amalekites live in the Negev region, the Hittites, Jebusites, and Amorites live in the hill country, and the Canaanites live by the sea and along the Jordan River.»
13.30: Caleb silenced the people concerning Moses: «Let us go up,» he said, “and take possession of the land, for we can take possession of it.”
13.31: But the men who had gone up with him said, "We are not able to go up against this people; they are stronger than we are."«
13.32: And they denounced before the children of Israel the land they had explored, saying, «The land we explored is a land that devours its inhabitants; all the people we saw there were of great stature,
13.33: And there we saw the giants, the sons of Enach, of the race of giants: we were in our own eyes, and in theirs, like grasshoppers.»

14.1: The whole assembly raised their voices and shouted, and the people wept throughout that night.
14.2: All the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron, and the whole assembly said to them, «If only we had died in the land of Egypt, or if only we had died in this desert!”
14.3: Why is the Lord bringing us to this land, only to let us fall by the sword? Our wives and children will become plunder. Wouldn't it be better for us to return to Egypt?»
14.4: And they said to one another, «Let us appoint a leader and return to Egypt.»
14.5: Moses and Aaron fell face down to the ground in the presence of the entire assembled congregation of the children of Israel.
14.6: Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephone, two of those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes
14.7: And they spoke thus to the whole assembly of the children of Israel: «The land which we have traversed to explore is an excellent land.
14.8: If the Lord is pleased with us, he will bring us into that land and give it to us, a land flowing with milk and honey.
14.9: »Only do not rebel against the Lord or be afraid of the people of the land, for they will be food for us; their refuge has departed from them, and the Lord is with us. Do not be afraid of them.”
14.10: The whole assembly was talking about stoning them, when the glory of the Lord appeared over the tent of meeting in the sight of all the children of Israel.
14.11: And the Lord said to Moses, «How long will this people despise me? How long will they not believe in me, in spite of all the wonders I have done among them?”
14.12: I will strike him with plague and destroy him, and I will make you into a nation greater and more powerful than he.»
14.13: Moses said to the Lord, «The Egyptians have heard that by your power you brought this people up from among them, and they have told the inhabitants of this land.
14.14: They have all learned that you, Lord, are in the midst of this people, that you appear face to face, you, Lord, that your cloud stands over them and that you go before them by day in a pillar of cloud and by night in a pillar of fire.
14.15: If you kill this people as if they were one man, the nations that have heard of you will say:
14.16: The Lord did not have the power to bring this people into the land he had sworn to give them, therefore he caused them to perish in the wilderness.
14.17: Now that the power of the Lord has been shown to be great, as you have declared, saying:
14.18: The Lord is slow to anger and abounding in mercy, forgiving iniquity and sin, yet he does not leave the guilty unpunished; he punishes the children for the iniquity of the fathers, to the third and fourth generation.
14.19: "Forgive the iniquity of this people according to the greatness of your mercy, as you have forgiven this people from Egypt until now."»
14.20: And the Lord said, «I forgive, according to your request,
14.21: But I live, and the glory of the Lord will fill the whole earth.
14.22: All the men who have seen my glory and the wonders I performed in Egypt and in the desert, who have tested me ten times and have not listened to my voice,
14.23: None of them will see the land I swore to their fathers. None of those who despised me will see it.
14.24: But my servant Caleb, who had a different spirit and remained faithful to me, I will bring into the land he went to, and his descendants will possess it.
14.25: The Amalekite and the Canaanite live in the valley: tomorrow turn back and set out for the desert, on the road to the Red Sea.»
14.26: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
14.27: «How long will I put up with this wicked assembly that murmurs against me? I have heard the murmurs that the children of Israel utter against me.
14.28: Tell them: As surely as I live, says the Lord, I will do to you according to the words you have spoken to me.
14.29: Your corpses will fall in this desert. All of you who were counted in the census, from the age of twenty and above, and who murmured against me,
14.30: You will not enter the land where I swore to settle you, except for Caleb, son of Jephone, and Joshua, son of Nun.
14.31: And your grandchildren, of whom you said: They will become prey, I will bring them in, and they will know the land that you have despised.
14.32: Your corpses will fall in the desert
14.33: And your sons will lead their flocks in the desert for forty years, and they will bear the punishment for your unfaithfulness, until your corpses are consumed in the desert.
14.34: According to the forty days you took to explore the country, as many days, as many years you will bear your iniquities for forty years and you will know what my absence is.
14.35: I, the Lord, have spoken. This is what I will do to this wicked assembly that has risen up against me: they will be consumed in this wilderness, they will die there.»
14.36: The men whom Moses had sent to explore the land, and who, upon their return, had caused the whole assembly to murmur against him by describing the land,
14.37: These men, who had spoken ill of the country, died before the Lord, struck down by a plague.
14.38: Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephone, were the only ones left alive among those men who had gone to explore the land.
14.39: Moses reported these words to all the children of Israel, and the people were greatly distressed.
14.40: Having risen early in the morning, they went up to the top of the mountain, saying, "Here we are. We will go up to the place of which the Lord has spoken, for we have sinned."«
14.41: Moses said, «Why are you transgressing the Lord’s command? It will not succeed for you.”.
14.42: Do not go up, for the Lord is not among you. Do not let yourselves be defeated by your enemies.
14.43: For the Amalekite and the Canaanite are there before you, and you will fall by the sword, because you have turned away from the Lord; the Lord will not be with you.»
14.44: They persisted in climbing towards the mountaintop, but the ark of the covenant of the Lord and Moses did not move from the middle of the camp.
14.45: Then the Amalekite and the Canaanite who lived in that mountain went down, beat them and cut them to pieces as far as Hormah.

15.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
15.2: «Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land where you will dwell and which I give you,
15.3: and when you offer to the Lord a sacrifice made by fire, whether a burnt offering or a sacrifice, for the fulfillment of a vow or as a freewill offering, or at your appointed feasts, to produce with your oxen or sheep a pleasing aroma to the Lord,
15.4: He who makes his offering to the Lord shall offer as a sacrifice one-tenth of the finest flour kneaded with one-quarter of a hin of oil.,
15.5: You shall make a libation of a quarter of a hin of wine, with the burnt offering or the peace offering, for each lamb.
15.6: For a ram, you will make an offering of two-tenths of a gram of fine flour kneaded with a third of a gram of oil.,
15.7: and for the libation you shall offer a third of a hin of wine, as a pleasing aroma offering to the Lord.
15.8: If you offer a bull, whether as a burnt offering, a sacrifice, a vow fulfillment, or a peace offering to the Lord,
15.9: Along with the bull, three-tenths of a kilogram of fine flour kneaded with half a hen's weight of oil will be offered as an oblation.,
15.10: and you shall offer half a hin of wine as a libation offering: it is a sacrifice made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
15.11: This will be done for each ox, for each ram, for each lamb or kid.
15.12: Depending on the number of victims you offer, you will do the same for each one, according to their number.
15.13: Every Israelite by birth shall do these things in this way, when he offers a sacrifice by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
15.14: If a stranger residing among you, any man living among you throughout your generations, offers a sacrifice by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord, he shall do as you do.
15.15: There shall be one law for the assembly, for you as for the stranger residing among you, it shall be a perpetual law for your descendants: it shall be with the stranger as with you before the Lord.
15.16: There will be one law and one rule for you and for the foreigner residing among you.»
15.17: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
15.18: «Speak to the children of Israel and tell them: When you arrive in the land to which I am bringing you,
15.19: and when you eat bread from this land, you shall set aside an offering for the Lord.
15.20: As a first offering of your dough, you shall set aside a cake, as an offering is set aside from the threshing floor.
15.21: As the firstfruits of your dough, you and your descendants shall set aside an offering for the Lord.»
15.22: «If you sin unintentionally by not observing all these commandments that the Lord revealed to Moses,
15.23: everything that the Lord commanded you through Moses, from the day the Lord gave the commandments and onward throughout your generations,
15.24: If, I say, they have sinned by mistake, without the assembly having noticed it, the whole assembly shall offer a young bull as a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma to the Lord, with its grain offering and its drink offering, according to the prescribed rite, as well as a male goat as a sin offering.
15.25: The priest shall make atonement for the whole congregation of the children of Israel, and they shall be forgiven, for it is a sin committed unintentionally, and they have presented their offering, a sacrifice made by fire to the Lord, and their sin offering before the Lord, because of the sin which they have committed unintentionally.
15.26: The whole assembly of the children of Israel and the stranger who sojourns among them will be forgiven, for it is in error that all the people have sinned.
15.27: If it is only one person who has sinned unintentionally, he shall offer a one-year-old goat as a sin offering.
15.28: The priest will make atonement before the Lord for the person who erred by committing a sin by mistake; once atonement has been made for him, he will be forgiven.
15.29: For the Israelite by birth among the children of Israel and for the stranger who sojourns among them, you shall have the same law if anyone does wrong by mistake.
15.30: But if anyone, whether an Israelite by birth or a foreigner, acts deliberately, he insults the Lord; that person shall be cut off from among his people.
15.31: For he has despised the word of the Lord and violated his commandment: this man shall be cut off, his iniquity shall be upon him.»
15.32: While the children of Israel were in the desert, they found a man gathering wood on the Sabbath day.
15.33: Those who found him gathering wood brought him to Moses, Aaron, and the whole assembly.
15.34: He was placed under guard, because what should be done to him had not yet been determined.
15.35: The Lord said to Moses, «This man shall be put to death. The whole assembly shall stone him outside the camp.»
15.36: The whole assembly brought him out of the camp, stoned him, and he died, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
15.38: «Speak to the children of Israel and tell them to make tassels for themselves, from generation to generation, on the corners of their robes, and to put on the tassel of each corner a purple cord.
15.39: This will be an acorn for you, and when you see it, you will remember all the commandments of the Lord to put them into practice, and you will not go after the desires of your heart and your eyes, which lead you into unfaithfulness.
15.40: You will then remember all my commandments and put them into practice, and you will be holy to your God.
15.41: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord your God.

16.1: Korah, son of Isaar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, gathered followers for himself, along with Dathan and Abiron, sons of Eliab, and Hon, son of Pheleth, these being sons of Reuben,
16.2: And they rose up in the presence of Moses, having with them two hundred and fifty men of the children of Israel, leaders of the assembly, called to the councils, and men of renown.
16.3: They gathered around Moses and Aaron and said to them, «Enough! For the whole assembly is holy, and the Lord is among them. Why then do you exalt yourselves above the assembly of the Lord?»
16.4: When Moses heard this, he fell face down to the ground.
16.5: He spoke to Korah and all his company, saying, «Tomorrow the Lord will reveal his holy one and bring him near to himself; he will bring near the one he chooses.
16.6: Do this: Take censers, Korah and all his company.
16.7: Tomorrow, set fire to it and sprinkle incense on it before the Lord. The one the Lord chooses is holy. That is enough, children of Levi.»
16.8: Moses said to Korah, «Listen now, children of Levi.
16.9: Is it too little for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the assembly of Israel, bringing you near to him to do the service of the tabernacle of the Lord and to stand before the assembly to do its service?
16.10: He has brought you near to him, you and all your brothers, the children of Levi, with you, and you still aspire to the priesthood.
16.11: That is why you and all your company are gathering against the Lord. And who is Aaron, that you should murmur against him?»
16.12: Moses summoned Dathan and Abiron, sons of Eliab, and they said, "We will not go up.
16.13: Isn't it enough that you brought us out of a land flowing with milk and honey only to make us die in the desert, that you set yourself up as master over us?
16.14: "Ah, it is not to a land flowing with milk and honey that you have led us, it is not fields and vineyards that you have given us as a possession. Do you think you can blind these people? We will not go up."»
16.15: Moses, very angry, said to the Lord, «Do not regard their offering. I have not taken even a donkey from them, nor have I harmed any of them.»
16.16: Moses said to Korah, «Tomorrow you and all your company, meet before the Lord, you and they, with Aaron.
16.17: Each of you shall take your censer, put incense in it, and each of you shall present your censer before the Lord: two hundred and fifty censers in total. You and Aaron shall each take your censer.»
16.18: They each took their censer, put fire in it and sprinkled incense on it, and they stood at the entrance of the tent of meeting, with Moses and Aaron.
16.19: Korah had summoned the whole assembly against Moses and Aaron at the entrance to the tent of meeting. Then the glory of the Lord appeared to all the assembly.
16.20: And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
16.21: «"Separate yourselves from the middle of this assembly and I will exterminate them in an instant."»
16.22: And they fell facedown to the ground and said, «O God, God of the spirits of all flesh, one man has sinned, and you would be angry with the whole assembly.»
16.23: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
16.24: «Speak to the assembly and say: »Depart from all around the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiron.’”
16.25: Moses got up and went to Dathan and Abiron, and some of the elders of Israel went after him.
16.26: He spoke to the assembly, saying, «Get away from the tents of these wicked men and do not touch anything that belongs to them, lest you perish, enveloped in all their sins.»
16.27: They withdrew all around the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiron. Then Dathan and Abiron went out and stood at the entrance of their tents, with their wives, their sons, and their grandchildren.
16.28: Moses said, «This is how you will know that the Lord has sent me to do all these works, and I am not doing them on my own:
16.29: If these people die as all men die, and if their fate is that of all men, then it is not the Lord who sent me.,
16.30: But if the Lord does something unprecedented, if the earth opens its mouth and swallows them up, along with all who belong to them, and they go down alive into the realm of the dead, then you will know that these people have despised the Lord.»
16.31: As he finished speaking all these words, the ground beneath them split open.
16.32: The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up, along with their families, all the people of Korah, and all their possessions.
16.33: They went down alive into the realm of the dead, they and all that belonged to them, and the earth covered them, and they disappeared from the midst of the assembly.
16.34: All Israel, who were around them, fled at their cry, for they said, «Let us flee, lest the earth swallow us up.»
16.35: Fire came out from the Lord and consumed the two hundred and fifty men who were offering incense.

17.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
17.2: «"Tell Eleazar, son of Aaron, the priest, to take the censers out of the midst of the blazing fire and to scatter the fire far away, for they are consecrated.
17.3: From the censers of these people who sinned against their own lives, let them be made into broad sheets to cover the altar of burnt offerings, for they were presented before the Lord and were made holy; they will serve as a sign to the children of Israel.»
17.4: The priest Eleazar took the bronze censers that the men consumed by the fire had presented and made blades out of them to cover the altar.
17.5: It is a memorial for the children of Israel, so that no foreigner, who is not of the race of Aaron, may approach to offer incense before the Lord and be like Korah and his company, according to what the Lord had declared to him through Moses.
17.6: The next day, the whole assembly of the children of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron, saying, "You have killed the Lord's people."«
17.7: As the assembly gathered against Moses and Aaron, they turned towards the tent of meeting and behold, the cloud covered it and the glory of the Lord appeared.
17.8: Moses and Aaron approached the tent of meeting,
17.9: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
17.10: «Get away from the midst of this assembly and I will consume them in an instant.» They fell face down to the ground,
17.11: And Moses said to Aaron, «Take the censer and put fire in it from the altar, and throw incense into it. Go quickly to the congregation and make atonement for them, for wrath has gone out from before the Lord; the plague has begun.»
17.12: Aaron took the censer, as Moses had instructed, and ran into the midst of the assembly, and behold, the plague began among the people. Having put the incense on them, he made atonement for the people.
17.13: He placed himself between the dead and the living, and the plague was stopped.
17.14: Fourteen thousand seven hundred people died from this plague, besides those who had died because of Korah.
17.15: Aaron returned to Moses at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and the plague was stopped.
