17.16: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
17.17: «Speak to the children of Israel and take from them a staff, one staff for each patriarchal house, twelve staffs in all, one from each of their princes for their twelve families. You shall write the name of each one on his staff,
17.18: You shall write the name of Aaron on the staff of Levi, for there shall be one staff for each head of their families.
17.19: You will place them in the meeting tent, in front of the testimony, where I meet with you.
17.20: The man I choose will be the one whose staff will flourish, and I will put an end to the murmuring of the Israelites against you.»
17.21: Moses spoke to the children of Israel and all their princes gave him a staff, each prince a staff, according to their families, twelve staffs in total, and Aaron's staff was in the middle of their staffs.
17.22: Moses laid the staffs before the Lord in the tent of the testimony.
17.23: The next day Moses returned to the tent of testimony and behold, Aaron’s staff had blossomed for the tribe of Levi: buds had sprouted on it, flowers had opened, and almonds had ripened.
17.24: Moses took all the staffs from before the Lord to all the children of Israel, and they saw them, and each one took back his staff.
17.25: The Lord said to Moses, «Put Aaron’s staff back before the Testimony, to be kept as a sign for the rebellious children, so that you may put an end to their grumbling before me, and they will not die.»
17.26: Moses did so, he did according to the command that the Lord had given him.
17.27: The children of Israel said to Moses, «Behold, we are perishing, we are lost, all lost.
17.28: "Whoever approaches the tabernacle of the Lord dies. Must we all perish?"»

18.1: The Lord said to Aaron, «You and your sons and your father’s house with you shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary, you and your sons with you shall bear the iniquity of your priesthood.
18.2: Also bring with you to the sanctuary your other brothers, the tribe of Levi, your father’s tribe, so that they may assist you and serve you when you and your sons with you are before the tent of testimony.
18.3: They will perform your service and the service of the whole tent, but they will not approach the utensils of the sanctuary or the altar, lest both they and you die.
18.4: They will be assigned to you and will perform the duties of the tent of meeting, for all the work of the tent. No stranger shall approach you.
18.5: You shall perform the service of the sanctuary and the service of the altar, so that there may be no more wrath against the children of Israel.
18.6: Behold, I have taken your brothers the Levites from among the children of Israel, who were given to the Lord; they are given to you as a gift to do the work of the tent of meeting.
18.7: You and your sons with you will fulfill your priesthood in everything concerning the altar and what is within the veil: you will do this work. I appoint you priests as a gift. Any foreigner who approaches will be put to death.»
18.8: The Lord said to Aaron, «Behold, I give you the service of what is set aside for me, of all the consecrated things of the children of Israel, I give them to you, by reason of the anointing which you have received, to you and to your sons, by a perpetual statute.
18.9: This is what will be yours of the most holy things, except what is to be consumed by fire: all their offerings, namely all their oblations, all their sin offerings and all their guilt offerings which they shall render to me: all these things, as most holy things, shall be for you and for your sons.
18.10: You shall eat them in a most holy place; every male shall eat of them, and they shall be holy to you.
18.11: This too belongs to you: whatever is taken from their gifts, from every offering waved from the children of Israel, I give to you, to your sons and to your daughters with you, by a perpetual statute, whoever is clean in your house shall eat of it.
18.12: All the best of the oil, all the best of the new wine and of the wheat, their first fruits which they offer to the Lord, I give to you.
18.13: The first fruits of their land that they bring to the Lord will be yours. Everyone in your household who is clean may eat of them.
18.14: Everything that is devoted by anathema in Israel will be yours.
18.15: Every firstborn of all flesh, whether human or animal, that they offer to the Lord, shall be yours. Only you shall redeem the firstborn of man and the firstborn of an unclean animal.
18.16: As for his redemption, you shall redeem him when he is one month old, according to your valuation, for five shekels of silver, according to the sanctuary shekel, which is twenty gerahs.
18.17: But you shall not redeem the firstborn of an ox, nor the firstborn of a sheep, nor the firstborn of a goat: they are holy. You shall sprinkle their blood on the altar and burn their fat as a burnt offering, an aroma pleasing to the Lord.
18.18: Their flesh will be yours, like the breast that is swung and like the right thigh.
18.19: »All that is taken from the holy things, which the children of Israel take for the Lord, I give to you, to your sons and to your daughters with you, by a perpetual statute, it is a perpetual covenant of salt before the Lord, for you and for your descendants with you.”
18.20: The Lord said to Aaron, «You will have no inheritance in their land and there will be no portion for you among them; I am your portion and your inheritance among the children of Israel.
18.21: Behold, I give to the sons of Levi all the tithes in Israel as an inheritance, for the work which they do, the work of the tent of meeting.
18.22: The children of Israel shall no longer approach the tent of meeting, lest they bear their sin and die.
18.23: The Levites will do the work of the tent of meeting and bear their iniquity. By a perpetual statute among your descendants, they will have no inheritance among the children of Israel.
18.24: For I give to the Levites as an inheritance the tithes that the children of Israel shall set aside for the Lord; therefore I say to them: They shall have no inheritance among the children of Israel.»
18.25: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
18.26: «You shall speak to the Levites and say to them, «When you receive from the children of Israel the tithe that I give you from their possessions for your inheritance, you shall set aside an offering to the Lord, a tithe of the tithe,
18.27: and this portion that you will take will be counted to you as the wheat that is taken from the threshing floor and as the new wine that is taken from the vat.
18.28: This is how you too shall set aside an offering for the Lord from all the tithes which you receive from the children of Israel, and this offering which you set aside for the Lord you shall give to Aaron the priest.
18.29: From all the gifts you receive, you shall take the whole offering of the Lord, and from all the best, the holy portion which is taken from it.
18.30: You shall tell them: When you have taken the best of it, the tithe shall be counted to the Levites as the produce of the threshing floor and as the produce of the winepress.
18.31: You and your family will eat it wherever you go, for it is your wages for the work you do in the tent of meeting.
18.32: You will not bear any sin for this, when you have taken the best of it; you will not profane the holy offerings of the children of Israel, and you will not die.»

19.1: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
19.2: «This is the ordinance of the law which the Lord has prescribed, saying: Tell the children of Israel to bring you a red heifer, without blemish, without defect in its body, and which has not borne the yoke.
19.3: You will hand it over to the priest Eleazar, who will take it out of the camp and it will be slaughtered in front of him.
19.4: The priest Eleazar will take some of the cow's blood with his finger and sprinkle this blood seven times on the side of the entrance to the tent of meeting.
19.5: The cow will be burned before his eyes, its skin, its flesh and its blood, as well as its excrement.
19.6: The priest will take cedar wood, hyssop, and crimson and throw them into the middle of the fire that is consuming the cow.
19.7: The priest shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, then he shall return to the camp and be unclean until evening.
19.8: The one who has burned the cow shall wash his clothes in water and bathe his body in water and he shall be unclean until evening.
19.9: A clean man shall collect the ashes of the heifer and deposit them outside the camp, in a clean place, and they shall be kept for the congregation of the children of Israel, for the water which removes defilement: it is a sin offering.
19.10: Anyone who gathers the ashes of the heifer must wash their clothes and be unclean until evening. This is a perpetual statute for the Israelites and for any foreigner residing among them.
19.11: Anyone who touches a dead body, any human body, will be unclean for seven days.
19.12: He shall purify himself with this water on the third day and the seventh day and he shall be clean, but if he does not purify himself on the third day and the seventh day, he shall not be clean.
19.13: Whoever touches a dead body, the body of a person who has died, and does not purify themselves, defiles the tabernacle of the Lord; that person shall be cut off from Israel. Because the water that removes defilement has not been sprinkled on them, they are unclean, and their defilement remains on them.
19.14: This is the law. When a man dies in a tent, whoever enters the tent and everything in the tent will be unclean for seven days.
19.15: Any uncovered vessel, to which there is no lid attached, is impure.
19.16: Anyone who touches in the field a man killed by the sword, or a dead body, or human bones, or a tomb, will be unclean for seven days.
19.17: For the one who is unclean, some of the ashes of the victim consumed as a sin offering will be taken, and fresh water will be poured over them in a vessel.
19.18: A clean man shall take hyssop and, having dipped it in water, shall sprinkle it on the tent, on all the utensils and on the people who were there, on the one who touched bones, or a slain man, or a dead man, or a tomb.
19.19: The clean person shall sprinkle the unclean person on the third day and the seventh day, and on the seventh day he shall remove their sin. The unclean person shall wash their clothes and bathe in water, and by evening they shall be clean.
19.20: The man who becomes unclean and does not purify himself shall be cut off from the midst of the assembly, for he defiles the sanctuary of the Lord, because the water that removes defilement has not been sprinkled on him; he is unclean.
19.21: This shall be a perpetual statute for them. Whoever sprinkles the water that removes uncleanness shall wash his clothes, and whoever touches the water that removes uncleanness shall be unclean until evening.
19.22: "Everything that an unclean person touches will become unclean, and the person who touches it will remain unclean until evening."»

20.1: The children of Israel, the whole assembly, arrived at the Desert of Sin in the first month, and the people stayed at Kadesh. It was there that Miriam died and was buried.
20.2: Since there was no water for the assembly, they gathered against Moses and Aaron.
20.3: The people argued with Moses and said, «Why did we not perish when our brothers perished before the Lord?”
20.4: Why have you brought the Lord's assembly into this wilderness, so that we and our livestock should die here?
20.5: »Why did you bring us up out of Egypt to bring us to this place of misery? It is not a place where we can sow seeds, and there are no fig trees, no vines, no pomegranates, and not even water to drink?”
20.6: Moses and Aaron, leaving the assembly, came to the entrance of the tent of meeting. They fell face down on the ground and the glory of the Lord appeared to them.
20.7: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
20.8: «Take the staff and gather the assembly; you and your brother Aaron shall speak to the rock in their presence, so that it may give its water. You shall bring water out of the rock for them and give it to the assembly and their livestock to drink.»
20.9: Moses took the staff that was before the Lord, as the Lord had commanded him.
20.10: Then Moses and Aaron summoned the assembly to the rock, and Moses said to them, «Listen, you rebels! Shall we bring you out of the water from this rock?»
20.11: Moses raised his hand and struck the rock twice with his staff, and abundant water gushed out. The assembly and the livestock drank.
20.12: Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron, «Because you did not believe in me to sanctify me in the sight of the Israelites, you will not bring this community into the land I am giving them.»
20.13: These are the waters of Meribah, where the children of Israel quarreled with the Lord, and he sanctified himself in them.
20.14: From Kadesh, Moses sent messages to the king of Edom, saying, «Thus says your brother Israel: You know all the sufferings we have endured.
20.15: Our fathers went down to Egypt and we stayed there a long time, but the Egyptians mistreated us and our fathers.
20.16: We cried out to the Lord and he heard our voice; he sent an angel and brought us out of Egypt. And now we are in Kadesh, a city on the border of your territory.
20.17: »Please let us pass through your country; we will not cross through fields or vineyards, nor will we drink water from wells, but we will follow the royal highway, without turning to the right or left, until we have crossed your territory.”
20.18: Edom said to him, "You shall not pass through my house, or I will come out against you with the sword."«
20.19: The Israelites said to him, «We will go up by the highway, and if we and my flocks drink your water, I will pay for it. It’s no trouble; I will only pass through on foot.»
20.20: He replied, "You shall not pass." And Edom came out to meet him with a large and strong army.
20.21: This is why Edom refused to allow Israel to pass through its territory, and Israel turned away from it.
20.22: The children of Israel, the whole assembly, set out from Kadesh and arrived at Mount Hor.
20.23: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron at Mount Hor, on the border of the land of Edom:
20.24: «Aaron will be gathered to his people, for he will not enter the land that I am giving to the children of Israel, because you rebelled against my command at the waters of Meribah.
20.25: Take Aaron and his son Eleazar and bring them up Mount Hor.
20.26: You shall strip Aaron of his clothes and put them on Eleazar his son. There Aaron shall be gathered and die.»
20.27: Moses did as the Lord had commanded; they went up Mount Hor in the sight of all the assembly.,
20.28: Then Moses, having stripped Aaron of his clothes, had Eleazar, his son, put them on, and Aaron died there, on the top of the mountain, and Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain.
20.29: The whole assembly saw that Aaron had died, and all the house of Israel mourned for Aaron for thirty days.

21.1: The Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negev, learned that Israel was coming by way of Atharim. He fought against Israel and took prisoners.
21.2: Then Israel made a vow to the Lord, saying, «If you deliver this people into my hands, I will devote their cities to destruction.»
21.3: The Lord heard the voice of Israel and delivered up the Canaanites; they devoted them and their cities to destruction, and the place was named Hormah.
21.4: They set out from Mount Hor along the road to the Red Sea, to go around the land of Edom. The people grew impatient on this journey,
21.5: And he spoke against God and against Moses: «Why did you bring us up out of Egypt to die in the desert? There is no bread, there is no water, and our souls loathe this miserable food.»
21.6: Then the Lord sent fiery serpents against the people, and they bit the people, and many people in Israel died.
21.7: The people came to Moses and said, «We have sinned, for we have spoken against the Lord and against you. Pray to the Lord that he will take these snakes away from us.» Moses prayed for the people.
21.8: And the Lord said to Moses, «Make a fiery serpent and place it on a pole; whoever is bitten and looks at it shall live.»
21.9: Moses made a bronze serpent and placed it on a pole, and if anyone was bitten by a serpent, he would look at the bronze serpent and live.
21.10: The children of Israel set out and they camped at Oboth.
21.11: They set out from Oboth and camped at Jeabarim, in the desert opposite Moab, toward the rising sun.
21.12: They left from there and camped in the Zared Valley.
21.13: They set out from there and camped beyond the Arnon, which flows in the desert, leaving the territory of the Amorites, for the Arnon is the border of Moab, between Moab and the Amorites.
21.14: That is why it is said in the Book of the Wars of the Lord: «The Lord took Vaheb in his impetuous course and the torrents of the Arnon,
21.15: and the slope of the streams which extends towards the site of Ar and abuts the border of Moab.»
21.16: From there they went to Beer. This is the well about which the Lord said to Moses, «Gather the people together and I will give them water.»
21.17: Then Israel sang this song: Arise, O well. Acclaim him.
21.18: The well, which princes dug, which the great men of the people opened, with the scepter, with their staffs. From the desert they went to Matthana,
21.19: from Matthana to Nahaliel, from Nahaliel to Bamoth,
21.20: from Bamoth, to the valley which is in the fields of Moab, to the top of Phasgah, which overlooks the desert.
21.21: Israel sent messengers to Sihon, king of the Amorites, to tell him:
21.22: «Let me pass through your country; we will not stray into the fields or the vineyards, nor will we drink water from the wells. We will follow the royal highway until we have passed through your territory.»
21.23: Sehon did not allow Israel to pass through his territory; he gathered all his people and went out to meet Israel in the desert, came to Jasa, and gave battle to Israel.
21.24: Israel struck him with the edge of the sword and took possession of his land from the Arnon to the Jaboc, as far as the Ammonites, for the border of the Ammonites was strong.
21.25: Israel took all these cities and Israel settled in all the cities of the Amorites, in Hezebon and in all the cities within its jurisdiction.
21.26: For Hesebon was the city of Sihon, king of the Amorites, who had made war on the previous king of Moab and had taken all his country as far as the Arnon.
21.27: That is why the poets say: Come to Hezebon. Let the city of Sehon be rebuilt and fortified.
21.28: For fire came out from Hezebon, a flame from the city of Sehon, and it devoured Ar-Moab. The rulers of the heights of the Arnon.
21.29: Woe to you, Moab! You are lost, people of Hamos! He has given his fugitive sons and captive daughters to Sihon, king of the Amorites.
21.30: And we launched our arrows at them, Hesebon is destroyed as far as Dibon, we devastated as far as Nophe, with fire as far as Medaba.
21.31: Israel settled in the land of the Amorites.
21.32: Moses sent to reconnoiter Jazer and they took the cities under his jurisdiction and expelled the Amorites who were there.
21.33: Then, changing direction, they went up the road to Bashan. Og, king of Bashan, came out to meet them, with all his people, to fight them at Edrai.
21.34: The Lord said to Moses, «Do not be afraid of him, for I have given him into your hands, along with all his people and his land. You shall do to him as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon.»
21.35: And they defeated him and his sons and all his people, until they left him no survivors, and they took possession of his country.

22.1: The children of Israel, having departed, camped in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan, opposite Jericho.
22.2: Balach, son of Zippor, saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites,
22.3: And Moab was very afraid of the people, for they were numerous; he was terrified of the children of Israel.
22.4: Moab said to the elders of Midian, "This multitude will devour everything around us, as an ox devours the green plants of the field." Balak, son of Zippor, was then king of Moab.
22.5: He sent messengers to Balaam son of Beor, to Pethor, which is on the River, in the land of the sons of his people, to summon him and say, «Behold, a people has come out of Egypt, they cover the face of the earth and they dwell opposite me.
22.6: »Come then, I pray you, curse this people for me, for they are more powerful than I; perhaps then I may defeat them and drive them out of the land, for I know that he whom you bless is blessed and he whom you curse is cursed.”
22.7: The elders of Moab and Midian set out, taking the diviner's fee with them. When they arrived at Balaam's house, they reported to him the words of Balak.
22.8: Balaam said to them, "Spend the night here and I will give you an answer, according to what the Lord tells me." And the princes of Moab stayed with Balaam.
22.9: God came to Balaam and said to him, "Who are these men you have with you?"«
22.10: Balaam answered God, «Balach son of Zippor, king of Moab, sent them to tell me:
22.11: »Look, the people who came out of Egypt cover the face of the earth. Come now, curse them for me, so that perhaps I may be able to fight against them and drive them out.”
22.12: God said to Balaam, «You shall not go with them, nor shall you curse this people, for they are blessed.»
22.13: In the morning, Balaam got up and said to the princes of Balak, «Go back to your own country, for the Lord refuses to let me go with you.»
22.14: Then the princes of Moab got up and went back to Balak and said, "Balaam refused to come with us."«
22.15: Balac sent princes again, in greater numbers and of higher rank than the first.
22.16: When they arrived at Balaam's side, they said to him, "This is what Balak son of Zippor says: 'Please let nothing prevent you from coming to me,
22.17: "For I will give you the greatest honors and I will do everything you tell me. But come, I beg you, curse this people for me."»
22.18: Balaam answered and said to Balak's servants, "Even if Balak were to give me all his house full of silver and gold, I could not transgress the command of the Lord my God by doing anything, whether small or great.
22.19: Now, I beg you, stay here too tonight, so that I may know what the Lord will tell me next.»
22.20: God came to Balaam at night and said to him, «If these men have come to call you, get up and go with them, but you shall do only what I tell you.»
22.21: Balaam got up in the morning and, having saddled his donkey, he set out with the princes of Moab.
22.22: God's anger was kindled because he was going, and the angel of the Lord stood in the way to stop him. Balaam was riding on his donkey, and he had his two servants with him.
22.23: The donkey saw the angel of the Lord standing in the road with his drawn sword in his hand, and she turned away from the road and went into the fields, and Balaam struck the donkey to bring her back to the road.
22.24: Then the angel of the Lord stood in a hollow way among the vineyards, where there was a fence on each side.
22.25: Seeing the angel of the Lord, the donkey pressed herself against the wall and pressed Balaam's foot against the wall, and he struck her again.
22.26: The angel of the Lord went on and stood in a narrow place where there was no room to turn to the right or to the left.
22.27: The donkey, seeing the angel of the Lord, lay down under Balaam, and Balaam's anger was kindled, and he struck the donkey with his staff.
22.28: The Lord opened the donkey's mouth, and she said to Balaam, "What have I done to you, that you have struck me these three times?"«
22.29: Balaam answered the donkey, "It's because you've tricked me; if I had a sword in my hand, I would kill you right now."«
22.30: The donkey said to Balaam, "Am I not your donkey, whom you have always ridden until now? Have I been accustomed to acting like this towards you?" And he replied, "No."«
22.31: The Lord opened Balaam's eyes and Balaam saw the angel of the Lord standing in the road with a drawn sword in his hand, and he bowed down and prostrated himself with his face to the ground.
22.32: The angel of the Lord said to him, «Why have you struck your donkey these three times? Behold, I have come out to oppose you, for in my sight the way that you follow leads you to ruin.
22.33: The donkey saw me and turned away from me those three times; if she hadn't turned away, I would have killed you and spared her life.»
22.34: Balaam said to the angel of the Lord, «I have sinned, for I did not know that you were standing in the road in front of me; and now, if it displeases you, I will turn back.»
22.35: The angel of the Lord said to Balaam, «Go with these men, but you shall say nothing other than what I tell you.» So Balaam went with the princes of Balak.
22.36: Balak, having heard that Balaam was coming, went out to meet him as far as the city of Moab, which is on the border of the Arnon, at the very edge of the border.
22.37: Balak said to Balaam, «Did I not already send to you to summon you? Why have you not come to me? Can I not truly treat you with honor?»
22.38: Balaam said to Balak, «Here I am, I have come to you, but now what can I say? I will only speak the words that God puts in my mouth.»
22.39: Balaam set out with Balak and they arrived at Qiriath-Chutsoth.
22.40: Balak sacrificed oxen and sheep and sent portions of them to Balaam and the princes who were with him.
22.41: In the morning, Balak took Balaam with him and brought him up to Bamoth-Baal, from where Balaam could see the last ranks of the people.

23.1: Balaam said to Balak, «Build me seven altars here and prepare seven bulls and seven rams for me here.»
23.2: Balak did as Balaam had said, and Balak and Balaam offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
23.3: Balaam said to Balak, «Stand by your burnt offering and I will go away; perhaps the Lord will come to meet me, and whatever he shows me I will tell you.» So he went up to a bare height.
23.4: God came to meet Balaam, who said to him, «I have erected the seven altars and have offered on each altar a bull and a ram.»
23.5: The Lord put a word in Balaam's mouth, saying, "Go back to Balak and speak to him like this."«
23.6: He returned to him and behold, Balak was standing by his burnt offering, he and all the princes of Moab.
23.7: And Balaam delivered his speech, saying: Balak brought me from Aram, the king of Moab brought me from the mountains of the East: «Come, curse Jacob for me. Come, denounce Israel.»
23.8: How can I curse when God does not curse? How can I be angry when the Lord is not angry?
23.9: For from the top of the rocks I see it, from the height of the hills I consider it: it is a people dwelling apart, and not to be numbered among the nations.
23.10: Who can count the dust of Jacob and enumerate a quarter of Israel? Let me die the death of the righteous, and let my end be like theirs.
23.11: Balak said to Balaam, "What have you done to me? I took you to curse my enemies, and look, you have only blessed them."«
23.12: He answered and said, «Should I not be careful to say what the Lord puts in my mouth?»
23.13: Balak said to him, "Come with me to another place, from where you will see it, you will only see the end of it, without seeing it all, and from there, curse it for me."«
23.14: He led him to the field of the Watchers, on the summit of Phasgah, and having built seven altars, he offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
23.15: And Balaam said to Balak, «Stand here by your burnt offering while I go there to meet God.»
23.16: The Lord came to meet Balaam and put a word in his mouth, saying, «Go back to Balak and say this.»
23.17: He returned to him, and behold, Balak was standing by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him. Balak said to him, «What did the Lord say?»
23.18: And Balaam delivered his speech, saying: Arise, Balak, and listen; give ear to me, son of Zippor:
23.19: God is not a man, that he should lie, nor a son of man, that he should repent. Does he speak and not do, or promise and not act?
23.20: Behold, I have received a command to bless, he has blessed: I will not revoke.
23.21: He sees no iniquity in Jacob, he sees no injustice in Israel. The Lord his God is with him; the acclamation of a king resounds within him.
23.22: God brought them out of Egypt; his strength was like that of a wild ox.
23.23: Because there is no magic in Jacob, nor divination in Israel, in his time it will be told to Jacob and to Israel what God wants to accomplish.
23.24: This is a people that rises like a lioness and stands like a lion; it does not lie down until it has devoured its prey and drunk the blood of the wounded.
23.25: Balak said to Balaam, "Do not curse him and do not bless him."«
23.26: Balaam answered and said to Balak, «Did I not tell you that I will do everything the Lord says?»
23.27: Balak said to Balaam, "Come now, I will take you to another place; perhaps it will please God that you curse him from there."«
23.28: Balak led Balaam to the top of Mount Phogor, which overlooks the desert.
23.29: And Balaam said to Balak, «Build me seven altars here and prepare for me here seven bulls and seven rams.»
23.30: Balak did as Balaam had said and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.

24.1: Balaam saw that it was pleasing to the Lord to bless Israel, and he did not go, as at other times, to meet the magic signs, but he turned his face toward the desert.
24.2: Looking up, he saw Israel encamped by tribes, and the Spirit of God was upon him.,
24.3: And he delivered his speech, saying: The oracle of Balaam son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is closed,
24.4: oracle of him who hears the words of God, who contemplates the vision of the Almighty, who falls and whose eyes are opened.
24.5: How beautiful are your tents, O Jacob, your dwellings, O Israel!.
24.6: They spread out like valleys, like gardens by a river, like aloes that the Lord planted, like cedars by the waters.
24.7: Water overflows from his two buckets, his race grows on abundant waters, his king rises above Agag and his kingdom is exalted.
24.8: God brought him out of Egypt and gave him the strength of a wild ox. He devoured the nations that waged war against him, shattering their bones and striking them down with his arrows.
24.9: He bends his knees, he lies down like a lion, like a lioness, who will make him rouse himself? Blessed is he who blesses you and cursed is he who curses you.
24.10: Balak's anger burned against Balaam and he clapped his hands and said to Balaam, "I called you to curse my enemies, and look, you have only blessed them these three times.
24.11: "Now flee, go home. I said I would honor you, but behold, the Lord has removed you from honor."»
24.12: Balaam answered Balak, «Did I not tell your messengers whom you sent me:
24.13: Even if Balak were to fill his house with silver and gold, I could not transgress the Lord's command by doing good or evil myself, but I would say whatever the Lord tells me.
24.14: And now, behold, I am going to my people; come then, let me tell you what this people will do to your people in the latter days.»
24.15: And Balaam delivered his speech and said: The oracle of Balaam, son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is closed,
24.16: oracle of him who hears the words of God, who knows the knowledge of the Most High, who contemplates the vision of the Almighty, who falls and whose eyes are opened.
24.17: I see him, but not as present; I behold him, but not near. A star shall come out of Jacob, a scepter shall rise out of Israel. He shall break in two the sides of Moab, he shall destroy all the sons of tumult.
24.18: Edom is his possession, Seir, his enemy, is his possession, and Israel displays its valor.
24.19: From Jacob comes a ruler, who destroys what remains of Edom in the cities.
24.20: Balaam saw Amalek and he delivered his speech and said: Amalek is the first of the nations and its end will be ruin.
24.21: Balaam saw the Kinnean and he delivered his speech and said: Your dwelling is secure and your nest is set upon the rock.
24.22: However, the Cinéan will continue to burn himself out, but for how long? Assur will take him into captivity.
24.23: Balaam delivered his speech and said: Alas! Who will survive when God does this?
24.24: Ships come from Citthim, they oppress Assyria, they oppress Eber, and it too is doomed to ruin.
24.25: Balaam got up, set out, and returned home, and Balak also went his own way.

25.1: While Israel remained in Settim, the people began to indulge in debauchery with the daughters of Moab.
25.2: They invited the people to sacrifice to their gods. And the people ate and bowed down before their gods.
25.3: Israel clung to Beelphegor, and the Lord's anger burned against Israel.
25.4: The Lord said to Moses, «Assemble all the leaders of the people and hang the guilty ones before the Lord in the face of the sun, so that the fire of the Lord’s anger may turn away from Israel.»
25.5: And Moses said to the judges of Israel, «Each of you must put to death those of your people who have joined themselves to Beelpegor.»
25.6: And behold, a man of the children of Israel came and brought to his brothers a Midianite woman, in the sight of Moses and in the sight of all the congregation of the children of Israel, who were weeping at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
25.7: At this sight, Phinehas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, rose from the midst of the assembly, took a spear in his hand,
25.8: He followed the Israelite man into the back tent and pierced them both, the Israelite man and the woman, through the stomach. Then the plague was stopped among the Israelites.
25.9: Twenty-four thousand died from the plague.
25.10: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
25.11: «Phinai, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, turned away my wrath from the children of Israel, because he was enraged by my jealousy in their midst, and I did not, in my jealousy, consume the children of Israel.
25.12: Therefore say: I grant him my covenant of peace:
25.13: This will be a covenant of a perpetual priesthood for him and his descendants after him, because he was zealous for his God and made atonement for the Israelites.»
25.14: The Israelite man who was killed with the Midianite woman was named Zamri, son of Salu; he was a prince of a patriarchal house of the Simeonites.
25.15: The Midianite woman who was killed was named Cozbi, daughter of Sur, a tribal chief, from a patriarchal house in Midian.
25.16: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
25.17: «Treat the Midianites as enemies and kill them,
25.18: for they acted as enemies toward you, seducing you with their cunning, through Phogor, through Cozbi, the daughter of a Midianite prince, their sister, who was killed on the day of the plague that occurred because of Phogor.»

26.1: Following this plague, the Lord spoke to Moses and to Eleazar, son of Aaron, the priest, saying:
26.2: «"Take a census of the entire congregation of the children of Israel, from twenty years old and above, according to their families, of all the men of Israel who are able to bear arms."»
26.3: So Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke to them in the plains of Moab, near the Jordan, opposite Jericho, saying:
26.4: «"You shall take a census of the people from twenty years old and above, as the Lord commanded Moses and the children of Israel when they came out of the land of Egypt."»
26.5: Reuben, the firstborn of Israel. Son of Reuben: of Enoch, of the family of the Enochites, of Phallu, of the family of the Phalluites,
26.6: of Hesron, the Hesronite family, of Charmi, the Charmite family.
26.7: These are the families of the Rubenites, their census number was forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty.
26.9: The sons of Eliab: Namuel, Dathan, and Abiron. It was this Dathan and Abiron, members of the council, who rebelled against Moses and Aaron, in the company of Korah, when it rebelled against the Lord.
26.10: The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up along with Korah, when the troop perished and the fire consumed the two hundred and fifty men: they served as an example.
26.11: But the sons of Korah did not die.
26.12: The sons of Simeon, according to their families: of Namuel, the family of the Namuelites; of Jamin, the family of the Jaminites; of Jachin, the family of the Jachinites,
26.13: From Zare the family of the Zareites, from Saul, the family of the Saulites.
26.14: These are the families of the Simeonites: twenty-two thousand two hundred.
26.15: The sons of Gad, according to their families: from Sephon, the family of the Sephonites; from Aggi, the family of the Aggites; from Sunit, the family of the Sunites,
26.16: From Ozni, the Oznite family, from Her, the Herite family,
26.17: From Arod, the family of the Arodites; from Ariel, the family of the Arielites.
26.18: These are the families of the sons of Gad, according to their census: forty thousand five hundred.
26.19: The sons of Judah: Her and Onan, but Her and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
26.20: These are the sons of Judah according to their families: from Sela, the family of the Seleitans; from Perez, the family of the Perezites; from Zerah, the family of the Zerahites.
26.21: The sons of Peres were: from Hesron, the family of the Hesronites, from Hamul, the family of the Hamulites.
26.22: These are the families of Judah, according to their census: seventy-six thousand five hundred.
26.23: The sons of Issachar, according to their families: from Tola, the family of the Tolaws; from Puah, the family of the Phaites;,
26.24: of Jasub, the family of the Jasubites, of Semran, the family of the Semranites.
26.25: These are the families of Issachar, according to their census: sixty-four thousand three hundred.
26.26: The sons of Zebulun according to their families: of Sared, the family of the Zaredites; of Elon, the family of the Elonites; of Jalel, the family of the Jaelites.
26.27: These are the families descended from Zebulun, according to their census: sixty thousand five hundred.
26.28: The sons of Joseph according to their families: Manasseh and Ephraim.
26.29: Son of Manasseh: of Machir, the family of the Machirites. Machir fathered Gilead, of Gilead, the family of the Gileadites.
26.30: These are the sons of Gilead: Jezer, from whom descended the family of the Jezerites; from Helech, the family of the Helites;,
26.31: Asriel, from whom descended the family of the Asrielites; Shechem, from whom descended the family of the Shechemites;,
26.32: Semida, from whom the Semidaite family descended; Hepher, from whom the Hephrite family descended.
26.33: Salphaad, son of Hepher, had no sons, but he had daughters. These are the names of Salphaad's daughters: Maalah, Noah, Heglah, Melchah, and Terah.
26.34: These were the families of Manasseh, their census number was fifty-two thousand seven hundred.
26.35: These are the sons of Ephraim according to their families: from Suthala, the family of the Suthalites; from Beher, the family of the Behrites; from Tehen, the family of the Tehenites.
26.36: These are the sons of Suthala: from Heran, the family of the Heranites.
26.37: These are the families of the sons of Ephraim, according to their census: thirty-two thousand five hundred. These are the sons of Joseph, according to their families.
26.38: The sons of Benjamin, according to their families: from Bela, the family of the Belites; from Asbel, the family of the Asbelites; from Ahiram, the family of the Ahiramites,
26.39: of Supham, the family of the Suphamites, of Hupham, the family of the Huphamites.
26.40: The sons of Bela were Hered and Noah; from Hered came the family of the Heredites; from Noah came the family of the Noemanites.
26.41: These were the sons of Benjamin, according to their families and their censuses, forty-five thousand six hundred.
26.42: These are the sons of Dan, according to their families: from Shuham came the family of the Shuhamites. These are the families of Dan, according to their families.
26.43: Total number of Suhamite families, according to their census: sixty-four thousand four hundred.
26.44: Sons of Asher, according to their families: of Jemna, the family of the Jemnites, of Jessui, the family of the Jessuits, of Brié, the family of the Briéites.
26.45: The sons of Brieh: from Heber, the family of the Hebrites, from Melchiel, the family of the Melchielites.
26.47: These are the families of Asher’s sons, according to their census: fifty-three thousand four hundred.
26.48: The sons of Naphtali, according to their families: of Jezeil, the family of the Jesielites; of Guni, the family of the Gunites
26.49: from Jezer, the family of the Jeserites, from Sellem, the family of the Selemites.
26.50: These are the families of Naphtali, according to their families and their censuses, there were forty-five thousand four hundred.
26.51: These were the children of Israel who were counted: six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty.
26.52: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
26.53: «"To these the country will be divided, to be their inheritance, according to the number of names.
26.54: To the more numerous you will give a greater inheritance and to the less numerous you will give a smaller inheritance; to each will be given their inheritance according to their census numbers.
26.55: The division of the land will be determined by lot. They will receive it in their share according to the names of the patriarchal tribes.
26.56: It is by chance that the inheritance will be distributed to the most numerous as well as to those who are fewer.»
26.57: These are the Levites who were counted according to their families: from Gerson, the family of the Gersonites; from Kaath, the family of the Kaathites; from Merari, the family of the Merarites.
26.58: These are the families of Levi, the family of the Lobnites, the family of the Hebronites, the family of the Moholites, the family of the Musites, and the family of the Korahites. Kohath fathered Amram,
26.59: and the name of Amram's wife was Jehoshebed, daughter of Levi, whom his mother bore to Levi in Egypt, she bore to Amram Aaron, Moses and Miriam, their sister.
26.60: To Aaron were born Nadab and Abiu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
26.61: Nadab and Abiu died when they brought foreign fire before the Lord.
26.62: Their census, all the males from the age of one month and above, numbered twenty-three thousand. For they were not included in the census of the children of Israel, because no inheritance was assigned to them among the children of Israel.
26.63: These are the men counted by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who took a census of the children of Israel in the plains of Moab, near the Jordan, opposite Jericho.
26.64: Among them were none of the children of Israel whom Moses and Aaron the priest had counted in the desert of Sinai,
26.65: for the Lord had said of them, «They shall die in the wilderness,» and not one of them remained except Caleb, son of Jephone, and Joshua, son of Nun.

27.1: Then the daughters of Salphaad, son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from the families of Manasseh, the son of Joseph, approached; their names were Maala, Noah, Heglah, Melchah, and Terah.
27.2: They presented themselves before Moses, before Eleazar the priest, and before the leaders of the whole assembly, at the entrance to the tent of meeting, and they said:
27.3: «Our father died in the desert; he was not among the company of those who conspired against the Lord, among the company of Korah, but he died for his sin and had no son.
27.4: Why should our father's name be cut off from his family because he had no sons? Give us some property from among our father's brothers.»
27.5: Moses brought their case before the Lord,
27.6: And the Lord said to Moses:
27.7: «The daughters of Salphaad have spoken a true truth. You shall give them as an inheritance property from among their father’s brothers and you shall pass on to them the inheritance from their father.
27.8: You shall speak to the children of Israel, saying: If a man dies without having a son, you shall transfer his inheritance to his daughter,
27.9: And if he has no daughter, you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.
27.10: If he has no brothers, you shall give his inheritance to his father's brothers.,
27.11: And if there are no brothers of his father, you shall give his inheritance to the nearest relative in his family, and he shall possess it. This shall be a ordinance for the children of Israel, as the Lord commanded Moses.»
27.12: The Lord said to Moses, «Go up to this mountain of Abarim and see the land that I am giving to the children of Israel.
27.13: You will see it, and you too will be gathered to your people, just as your brother Aaron was gathered.,
27.14: Because in the Desert of Sin, during the dispute with the assembly, you both rebelled against my command to consecrate myself before them at the waters. These are the waters of Meribah Kadesh, in the Desert of Sin.»
27.15: Moses spoke to the Lord, saying:
27.16: «May the Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the assembly.”
27.17: who goes out before them and who comes in before them, who leads them out and brings them in, so that the church of the Lord will not be like sheep without a shepherd.»
27.18: The Lord said to Moses, «Take Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom the Spirit dwells, and lay your hand on him.
27.19: You shall place it before Eleazar the priest and before all the assembly, and you shall set it in their sight.
27.20: You shall place a portion of your authority over him, so that all the congregation of the children of Israel may obey him.
27.21: He shall appear before Eleazar the priest, who shall inquire of the Lord for him in the judgment of the Urim. At his command he and Joshua shall go out, and at his command all the children of Israel with him and the whole assembly shall go in.»
27.22: Moses did as the Lord had commanded him. He took Joshua and placed him before Eleazar the priest and before all the assembly.
27.23: And having laid his hands on him, he installed him, as the Lord had said through Moses.

28.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
28.2: «Give this order to the children of Israel and tell them: You shall be careful to present to me, at the appointed time, my offering, my food for the sacrifices made by fire, which are a pleasing aroma to me.
28.3: You shall tell them: This is the sacrifice by fire that you shall offer to the Lord: every day, two lambs a year old, without blemish, as a perpetual burnt offering.
28.4: You will offer one of the lambs in the morning and the second lamb between the two evenings,
28.5: and, for the oblation, one tenth of an epha of flour, kneaded with a quarter of a hin of crushed olive oil.
28.6: This is the perpetual burnt offering that was offered on Mount Sinai, a pleasing aroma, a sacrifice by fire to the Lord.
28.7: His libation shall be a quarter of a hin for each lamb; in the holy place you shall pour out the libation of pure wine to the Lord.
28.8: You shall offer the second lamb between the two evenings, as with the morning offering and its drink offering: it is a sacrifice by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
28.9: On the Sabbath day, you shall offer two lambs a year old, without blemish, and as an offering, two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil and its libation.
28.10: This is the Sabbath burnt offering, for each Sabbath, besides the perpetual burnt offering and its libation.
28.11: At the beginning of your months, you shall offer as a burnt offering to the Lord two young bulls, one ram, and seven lambs a year old without blemish,
28.12: and, as an offering for each bull, three-tenths of the finest flour kneaded with oil, as an offering for the ram, two-tenths of the finest flour kneaded with oil,
28.13: as an offering for each lamb, a tenth of the finest flour mixed with oil. It is a burnt offering of pleasing aroma, a sacrifice by the fire of the Lord.
28.14: Their libations shall be half a hin of wine for a bull, a third of a hin for a ram, and a quarter of a hin for a lamb. This is the burnt offering at the beginning of the month, for each month, for the months of the year.
28.15: They will also offer to the Lord a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
28.16: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, it will be the Lord's Passover.
28.17: The fifteenth day of this month shall be a day of celebration. Unleavened bread shall be eaten for seven days.
28.18: On the first day there will be a holy assembly: you shall do no servile work.
28.19: You shall offer as a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls, one ram, and seven lambs a year old, without blemish,
28.20: and, as their offering, of the finest flour kneaded with oil: three-tenths for a bull, two-tenths for a ram,
28.21: and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
28.22: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, to make atonement for yourselves.
28.23: You will do this without prejudice to the morning holocaust, which is the perpetual holocaust.
28.24: You shall do this every day for seven days; it is the food of a sacrifice by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord, so it shall be done without prejudice to the perpetual burnt offering and its libation.
28.25: On the seventh day you shall have a holy assembly: you shall do no servile work.
28.26: On the day of firstfruits, when you present to the Lord an offering of the new harvest, at your Feast of Weeks, you shall have a holy assembly: you shall do no servile work.
28.27: You shall offer as a pleasing aroma burnt offering to the Lord two young bulls, one ram, and seven lambs a year old,
28.28: and, as their offering, of the finest flour kneaded with oil: three-tenths for each bull, two-tenths for the ram,
28.29: and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
28.30: You shall also offer a goat, to make atonement for yourselves. You shall do this without prejudice to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering.
28.31: You will have flawless victims and you will add their libations to them.

29.1: In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall have a holy assembly: you shall do no servile work. It shall be for you a day of the sounding of the trumpets.
29.2: You shall offer as a pleasing aroma to the Lord a young bull, a ram, and seven lambs a year old, without blemish,
29.3: and, as their offering, of the finest flour kneaded with oil: three-tenths for the bull, two-tenths for the ram
29.4: and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
29.5: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, to make atonement for yourselves.
29.6: You shall do this without prejudice to the monthly burnt offering and its grain offering, the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings according to the prescribed rules. These are offerings by fire, an aroma pleasing to the Lord.
29.7: On the tenth day of this seventh month, you shall have a holy assembly and you shall afflict your souls: you shall do no work.
29.8: You shall offer as a pleasing aroma to the Lord a young bull, a ram, and seven lambs a year old, without blemish,
29.9: and, as their offering, of the finest flour kneaded with oil: three-tenths for the bull, two-tenths for the ram
29.10: and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs.
29.11: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, without prejudice to the sin offering, the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and their drink offerings.
29.12: On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you shall have a holy assembly: you shall do no servile work. And you shall celebrate a festival to the Lord for seven days.
29.13: You shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord: thirteen young bulls, two rams, and fourteen lambs a year old, without blemish,
29.14: and, as their offering, of the finest flour kneaded with oil: three-tenths for each of the thirteen bulls, two-tenths for each of the two rams
29.15: and one-tenth for each of the fourteen lambs.
29.16: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, without prejudice to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and its drink offering.
29.17: On the second day, you will offer twelve young bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs, without defect,
29.18: with their offerings and libations, for the bulls, the rams and the lambs, according to their number, according to the rule.
29.19: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and their drink offerings.
29.20: On the third day, you will offer eleven bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish,
29.21: with their offerings and libations, for the bulls, the rams and the lambs, according to their number, according to the rule.
29.22: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
29.23: On the fourth day, you shall offer ten bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish,
29.24: with their offerings and libations, for the bulls, the rams and the lambs, according to their number according to the rule.
29.25: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
29.26: On the fifth day, you shall offer nine bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish,
29.27: with their offerings and libations, for the bulls, the rams and the lambs, according to their number, according to the rule.
29.28: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
29.29: On the sixth day, you shall offer eight bulls, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without blemish,
29.30: with their offerings and libations, for the bulls, the rams and the lambs, according to their number, according to the rule.
29.31: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
29.32: On the seventh day, you shall offer seven bulls, two rams, and fourteen one-year-old lambs without blemish,
29.33: with their offerings and libations, for the bulls, the rams and the lambs, according to their number, according to the rule.
29.34: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
29.35: On the eighth day you shall have a solemn assembly: you shall do no servile work.
29.36: You shall offer a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma made by fire to the Lord: one bull, one ram, and seven lambs a year old, without blemish,
29.37: with their offerings and libations, for the bull, the ram and the lambs, according to their number, according to the rule.
29.38: You shall also offer a goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
29.39: These are the sacrifices you shall offer to the Lord at your appointed feasts, in addition to your vows and freewill offerings: your burnt offerings, your grain offerings, your drink offerings, and your peace offerings.»

30.1: Moses spoke to the children of Israel according to all that the Lord had commanded him:
30.2: Moses spoke to the leaders of the tribes of the children of Israel, saying, «This is what the Lord commands:
30.3: If a man makes a vow to the Lord or takes an oath to bind himself to a covenant, he will not break his word; he will do everything that comes out of his mouth.
30.4: If a woman, still a virgin in her father's house, makes a vow to the Lord and binds herself by a covenant,
30.5: and that her father learns of her vow and the commitment she has made to herself, and that her father remains silent towards her, all the vows she has made and all the commitments she has made to herself will be valid.,
30.6: But if, on the day he learns of it, her father disowns her, all her vows and commitments that she has made to herself will be worthless, and the Lord will forgive her, because her father has disowned her.
30.7: If she marries and is burdened by her vows or by a word imprudently uttered by her lips by which she imposed a commitment upon herself,
30.8: And if her husband, upon learning of it, remains silent towards her on the day he finds out, her vows will be valid, as will the commitments she has made to herself.,
30.9: But if, on the day he learns of it, her husband disavows her, he nullifies her vow that weighs upon her and the word imprudently uttered by her lips by which she imposed upon herself a commitment, and the Lord will forgive her.
30.10: The vow of a widowed or repudiated woman, any commitment she has made to herself, will be valid for her.
30.11: If a woman made a vow or imposed a commitment upon herself by an oath in her husband's house,
30.12: and if her husband, upon learning of this, remains silent towards her and does not disavow her, all her vows will be valid, as well as all the commitments she has made to herself.,
30.13: But if, on the day she hears of them, her husband annuls them, then everything that has come out of her lips, vows or commitments, will be void: her husband has annulled them, and the Lord will forgive her.
30.14: Any vow or oath by which she undertakes to afflict his soul, her husband may ratify and her husband may annul.
30.15: If her husband remains silent towards her from one day to the next, he thereby ratifies all his vows or commitments that weigh upon her; he ratifies them because he remained silent towards her on the day he learned of them.
30.16: If he annuls them after the day he learns of them, he will bear the iniquity of his wife.»
30.17: These are the laws that the Lord prescribed to Moses, between a husband and his wife, and between a father and his daughter, while she is still young and in her father's house.

31.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
31.2: «"Avenge the children of Israel on the Midianites, and then you will be gathered to your people."»
31.3: Moses spoke to the people, saying, «Arm some of your men for war and let them march against Midian, to execute the Lord’s vengeance on Midian.
31.4: You shall send to war one thousand men per tribe, from all the tribes of Israel.»
31.5: So they raised from among the thousands of Israel a thousand men per tribe, that is, twelve thousand armed men for war.
31.6: Moses sent them into battle, a thousand men from each tribe, them and Phinehas, the son of Eleazar the priest, who had with him the sacred instruments and the resounding trumpets.
31.7: They advanced against Midian, according to the order that the Lord had given to Moses, and they killed all the males.
31.8: Besides those who had fallen in the battle, they killed the kings of Midian: Evi, Rechem, Shur, Hur and Rebe, five kings of Midian; they also killed Balaam, son of Beor, with the sword.
31.9: The children of Israel took captive the women of the Midianites with their grandchildren and they plundered all their beasts of burden, all their flocks and all their possessions.
31.10: They burned to the ground all the towns in the country they inhabited and all their camps.
31.11: Having taken all the plunder and spoils, both people and livestock,
31.12: They brought the prisoners, the spoils, and the plunder to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the assembly of the children of Israel, at the camp in the plains of Moab, near the Jordan, opposite Jericho.
31.13: Moses, Eleazar, the priest, and all the leaders of the assembly went out to meet them outside the camp.
31.14: And Moses became angry with the commanders of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who were returning from battle.
31.15: He said to them, "Have you then spared the lives of all the women?"
31.16: Behold, it was they who, at Balaam's word, led the children of Israel into unfaithfulness to the Lord in the matter of Phogor, and so the plague was in the assembly of the Lord.
31.17: Now kill every male among the little children and kill every woman who has known a man by lying with him,
31.18: But all the girls who haven't known a man by sleeping with him, let them live for you.
31.19: And you, camp outside the camp for seven days; whoever has killed anyone and whoever has touched a dead body shall purify himself on the third day and on the seventh day, he and your prisoners.
31.20: You shall also purify all garments, all articles of leather, all articles of goats' hair, and all utensils of wood.»
31.21: Eleazar the priest said to the warriors who had gone into battle, «This is what is commanded by the law that the Lord prescribed to Moses:
31.22: gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin and lead,
31.23: Anything that goes into the fire, you must pass through the fire, and it will be clean; however, it must be purified again with the water of purification. Anything that does not go into the fire, you must pass through water.
31.24: You will wash your clothes on the seventh day and you will be clean; then you may enter the camp.
31.25: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
31.26: «You, Eleazar the priest, and the heads of the houses of the assembly, should take an inventory of what has been taken from the spoils, both men and livestock.”
31.27: and divides the spoils between the combatants who went to war and the entire assembly.
31.28: You shall take from the share of the soldiers who went to war a tribute for the Lord, namely one out of every five hundred, people, oxen, donkeys and sheep.
31.29: You shall take it from their half and give it to Eleazar the priest as a contribution to the Lord.
31.30: From the half that belongs to the children of Israel, you shall set aside one out of every fifty people, oxen, donkeys, sheep, and all livestock, and you shall give it to the Levites, who are in charge of the tabernacle of the Lord.»
31.31: Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord had commanded Moses.
31.32: The spoils, the remainder of the loot taken by the combatants, amounted to six hundred and seventy-five thousand sheep.,
31.35: and thirty-two thousand of the women who had not known a man by sleeping with him.
31.36: Half, the share of those who had gone to war, was three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,
31.37: six hundred and seventy-five sheep for the Lord's tribute,
31.38: thirty-six thousand oxen, of which seventy-two were for the Lord's tribute,
31.39: thirty thousand five hundred donkeys, including sixty-one for the Lord's tribute,
31.40: and sixteen thousand people, thirty-two people for the tribute of the Lord.
31.41: Moses gave to Eleazar the priest the tribute set aside for the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
31.42: As for the half belonging to the children of Israel, which Moses had separated from that of the warriors,
31.43: This half, forming the share of the assembly, amounted to three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep.,
31.47: Of this half which belonged to the children of Israel, Moses took one out of fifty and gave it to the Levites, who have the care of the tabernacle of the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
31.48: The commanders of the thousands of the army, the leaders of thousands and the leaders of hundreds, approached Moses
31.49: and said to him, "Your servants have counted the fighting men who were under our command, and not one of us is missing.
31.50: That is why we bring as an offering to the Lord the gold articles that each of us has found—bracelets, chains, rings, earrings, and necklaces—to make atonement for us before the Lord.»
31.51: Moses and Eleazar the priest received from them this gold, all these finely crafted objects.
31.52: All the gold collected that they presented to the Lord on behalf of the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, amounted to sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels.
31.53: The men in the troop each received their own loot.
31.54: Moses and Eleazar the priest, having taken the gold from the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, brought it into the tent of meeting as a memorial for the children of Israel before the Lord.

32.1: The sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad had a great many flocks. Seeing that the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead were a suitable place for flocks,
32.2: The sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came to Moses, Eleazar the priest, and the leaders of the assembly, and they said to them
32.3: «Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nemra, Hesebon, Eleale, Saban, Nebo and Beon,
32.4: This land, which the Lord struck down before the assembly of Israel, is a place suitable for flocks, and your servants have flocks.».
32.5: They added, "If we have found favor in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants as a possession, and do not make us cross the Jordan."«
32.6: Moses answered the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben, «Will your brothers go to war and you stay here?
32.7: Why do you discourage the children of Israel from passing into the land that the Lord is giving them?
32.8: That is what your fathers did when I sent them from Cadez-Barné to explore the country.
32.9: They went up to the valley of Escol and saw the land, and they discouraged the children of Israel from entering the land that the Lord was giving them.
32.10: And the Lord's anger was kindled that day, and he swore, saying
32.11: These men who came up from Egypt, from the age of twenty and above, will not see the land that I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, because they did not faithfully follow me.,
32.12: except Caleb, son of Jephone the Cenezean, and Joshua, son of Nun, who faithfully followed the Lord.
32.13: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness for forty years, until the whole generation that had done evil in the sight of the Lord was destroyed.
32.14: And now you are taking the place of your fathers, a race of sinful men, to increase the Lord's anger even more against Israel.
32.15: For if you refuse to follow him, he will continue to abandon Israel in the desert, and you will bring ruin upon all these people.»
32.16: They approached Moses and said, «We will build here pastures for our flocks and cities for our grandchildren,
32.17: but we will arm ourselves without delay to march before the children of Israel, until we have brought them into the place which they are to occupy and our children will remain in the fortified cities, because of the inhabitants of the land.
32.18: We will not return to our homes until the children of Israel have each taken possession of their inheritance.,
32.19: for we will possess nothing with them on the other side of the Jordan, nor beyond, since our inheritance came to us on this side of the Jordan, to the east.»
32.20: Moses said to them, «If you continue in this way, if you arm yourselves to fight before the Lord,
32.21: if all your armed men cross the Jordan before the Lord, until he has driven his enemies out of his presence,
32.22: and that you do not return until after the land has been subdued before the Lord, you will be blameless before the Lord and before Israel, and this land will be your possession before the Lord.
32.23: But if you do not act in this way, behold, you are sinning against the Lord, and know that your sin will overtake you.
32.24: »Therefore build cities for your children and parks for your flocks, and do the word that has gone out from your mouth.”
32.25: The sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben spoke to Moses, saying, «Your servants will do as my lord commands.
32.26: Our children, our wives, our flocks, and all our livestock will remain here in the cities of Gilead.,
32.27: And your servants, every armed man ready for battle, will go before the Lord to fight, as my lord says.»
32.28: Then Moses gave orders concerning them to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua son of Nun, and to the heads of the families of the tribes of the children of Israel,
32.29: He said to them, «If the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben cross the Jordan with you, all the armed men ready to fight before the Lord, and the land is subdued before you, you shall give them the conquest of Gilead as their possession.
32.30: But if they do not go over armed with you, they will be settled among you in the land of Canaan.»
32.31: The sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben answered, saying, «What the Lord has said to your servants, we will do.
32.32: We will go under arms before the Lord in the land of Canaan, and the possession of our inheritance will remain with us on this side of the Jordan.»
32.33: Moses gave to the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben and to half the tribe of Manasseh, son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon, king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og, king of Bashan, the land with its cities and their territories, the cities of the surrounding land.
32.34: The sons of Gad built Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,
32.36: Bethnemra and Betharan, fortified cities, and they made enclosures for the flock.
32.37: The sons of Reuben built Hezebon, Elealeh, Caryathaim,
32.38: Nabo and Baalmeon, whose names were changed, and Sabama, and they gave names to the cities they built.
32.39: The sons of Machir, son of Manasseh, went against Gilead and, having captured it, they drove out the Amorites who were there.
32.40: Moses gave Gilead to Machir, son of Manasseh, who settled there.
32.41: Jair, son of Manasseh, went and took their towns and called them Jair's Towns.
32.42: Noah went and took possession of Chanath and the towns within its jurisdiction, and named it after himself.

33.1: These are the encampments of the children of Israel, when they came out of the land of Egypt, according to their divisions, under the leadership of Moses and Aaron.
33.2: Moses wrote down the places from which they departed, according to their encampments, according to the Lord's command, and these are their encampments according to their departures:
33.3: They left Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month. The day after Passover, the Israelites went out with their hands raised in the sight of all the Egyptians.
33.4: And the Egyptians buried those whom the Lord had struck down among them, all their firstborn; the Lord also executed judgments on their gods.
33.5: Having left Ramses, the children of Israel camped at Socoth.
33.6: They set out from Soccoth and camped at Etham, which is on the edge of the desert.
33.7: They set out from Etham and, having turned towards Phihahiroth, which is opposite Beel-sephon, they camped before Magdalum.
33.8: They set out from before Phihahiroth and crossed into the midst of the sea, towards the desert. After three days' march in the desert of Etham, they camped at Marah.
33.9: They left Marah and arrived at Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.
33.10: They set out from Elim and camped near the Red Sea.
33.11: They departed from the Red Sea and camped in the desert of Sin.
33.12: They departed from the Sin desert and camped at Daphca.
33.13: They left Daphca and camped at Alus.
33.14: They left Alus and camped at Raphidim, and there was no water there for the people to drink.
33.15: They left Raphidim and camped in the Sinai desert.
33.16: They departed from the Sinai desert and camped at Kibroth-Hattaava.
33.17: They departed from Kibroth-Hattaava and camped at Haseroth.
33.18: They departed from Haseroth and camped at Rethma.
33.19: They left Rethma and camped at Remmonphares.
33.20: They departed from Remmonphares and camped at Lebna.
33.21: They left Lebna and camped at Ressa.
33.22: They left Ressa and camped at Ceelatha.
33.23: They departed from Ceelatha and camped at the mountain of Sepher.
33.24: They set out from the mountain of Sepher and camped at Arada.
33.25: They left Arada and camped at Macelot.
33.26: They departed from Machalot and camped at Thahath.
33.27: They departed from Thahath and camped at Thare.
33.28: They departed from Thare and camped at Metcha.
33.29: They left Metcha and camped at Hesmona.
33.30: They left Hesmona and camped at Moséroth.
33.31: They left Moséroth and camped at Bené-Jaacan.
33.32: They left Bene-Jaacan and camped at Hor-Gadgad.
33.33: They departed from Hor-Gadgad and camped at Jetebatha.
33.34: They left Jetebathah and camped at Hebronah.
33.35: They departed from Hebron and camped at Asiongaber.
33.36: They left Asiongaber and camped in the desert of Sin, that is to say at Cadez.
33.37: They set out from Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor, at the edge of the land of Edom.
33.38: Aaron the priest went up Mount Hor at the Lord’s command, and there he died in the fortieth year after the Israelites came out of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first day of the month.
33.39: Aaron was one hundred and twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor.
33.40: It was then that the Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negev in the land of Canaan, learned of the arrival of the children of Israel.
33.41: They set out from Mount Hor and camped at Salmona.
33.42: They left Salmona and camped at Phunon.
33.43: They left Phunon and camped at Oboth.
33.44: They set out from Oboth and camped at Ije-Abarim, on the border of Moab.
33.45: They departed from Ije-Abarim and camped at Dibon-Gad.
33.46: They departed from Dibon-Gad and camped at Helmon-Deblathaim.
33.47: They set out from Helmon-Deblathaim and camped in the Abarim mountains, opposite Nebo.
33.48: They set out from the Abarim mountains and camped in the plains of Moab, near the Jordan, opposite Jericho.
33.49: They camped near the Jordan, from Bethsimoth to Abel Settim, in the plains of Moab.
33.50: The Lord spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan, opposite Jericho, saying
33.51: «Speak to the children of Israel: When you have crossed the Jordan and entered the land of Canaan,
33.52: You will expel before you all the inhabitants of the land, you will destroy all their carved stones and you will destroy all their cast bronze images and you will devastate all their high places.
33.53: You will take possession of the land and inhabit it, for I have given you the land to possess.
33.54: You will divide the land by lot, according to your families; you will give a larger inheritance to those who are more numerous and a smaller inheritance to those who are fewer. What the lot assigns to each will belong to them; you will receive it as your property, according to your ancestral tribes.
33.55: But if you do not expel the inhabitants of the land before you, those of them whom you leave will be like thorns in your eyes and like pricks in your sides, and they will treat you as enemies in the land that you are going to inhabit.
33.56: And I will treat you as I had resolved to treat them.»

34.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
34.2: «Command the children of Israel and tell them: When you enter the land of Canaan, this is the land that will fall to you as your inheritance: the land of Canaan according to its boundaries, namely:
34.3: Your southern border will be the Desert of Sin, as far as Edom, and your southern border will extend from the southern end of the Salt Sea, eastward.,
34.4: and your border will incline southward by the ascent of Akrabbim, pass through Sin and reach as far south as Kadesh-Barneh, it will continue through Hazar-Adar and pass towards Asemon,
34.5: and from Asemon, the border will reach the Torrent of Egypt, to reach the sea.
34.6: As for the western border, your border will be the great sea: that will be your western limit.
34.7: This will be your northern border: starting from the great sea, you will draw it for yourself along Mount Hor,
34.8: From Mount Hor, you will trace it to the entrance of Hamath, and the border will reach Sedada.,
34.9: and the border will continue through Zephron, to reach Hatsar-Enan: this will be your northern limit.
34.10: You will draw your eastern border from Hazar-Enan to Sepham.,
34.11: and the border will descend from Sepham to Rebla, east of Ain, and the border will descend and extend along the hills that flank the sea from Cenereth to the east,
34.12: and the border will run down the Jordan River to the Salt Sea. This will be your country, with its borders all around it.»
34.13: Moses gave this order to the children of Israel, saying: «This is the land that you shall divide by lot and that the Lord has commanded to be given to the nine tribes and the half-tribe.
34.14: For the tribe of the sons of Reuben, according to their families, and the tribe of the sons of Gad, according to their families, received their inheritance, and the half-tribe of Manasseh received its inheritance.
34.15: The two tribes and the half-tribe took their inheritance beyond the Jordan, opposite Jericho, on the east side, toward the rising sun.»
34.16: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
34.17: «These are the names of the men who will divide the land among you: Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun.
34.18: You will take one prince from each tribe to divide the country between you.
34.19: These are the names of these men: For the tribe of Judah, Caleb, son of Jephone,
34.20: for the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Samuel, son of Ammiud,
34.21: for the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad, son of Chaselon,
34.22: for the tribe of the sons of Dan, Prince Bocci, son of Jogli,
34.23: for the sons of Joseph: for the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, Prince Hanniel, son of Ephod,
34.24: and for the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, Prince Camuel, son of Sephtan,
34.25: for the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, Prince Elisaphan, son of Pernah,
34.26: for the tribe of the sons of Issachar, Prince Phaltiel, son of Ozan,
34.27: for the tribe of the sons of Asher, Prince Ahiud, son of Salomi,
34.28: for the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, Prince Phedael, son of Ammiud.»
34.29: These are the ones whom the Lord commanded to divide the land of Canaan among the children of Israel.

35.1: The Lord spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan River opposite Jericho, saying:
35.2: «Command the children of Israel to give to the Levites, from the inheritance they will possess, cities to live in. You shall also give the Levites places of pasture around these cities.
35.3: They will have cities to live in, and their pastures will be for their cattle, their other possessions, and all their animals.
35.4: The pasturelands of the cities that you will give to the Levites shall be, from the wall of the city, outside, for a thousand cubits all around.
35.5: You shall measure, outside the city, two thousand cubits for the east side, two thousand cubits for the south side, two thousand cubits for the west side and two thousand cubits for the north side, the city shall be in the middle: these shall be the grazing places of their cities.
35.6: As for the cities that you shall give to the Levites, they are the six cities of refuge, which you shall provide for the murderer to take refuge in, and in addition, you shall give forty-two other cities.
35.7: Thus all the cities that you will give to the Levites will be forty-eight cities with their places of pasture.
35.8: As for the towns you will give from the possessions of the Israelites, you will take more from those who have more and less from those who have less. Each will give some of their towns to the Levites in proportion to the inheritance they received.»
35.9: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
35.10: «Speak to the children of Israel and tell them: When you have crossed the Jordan and entered the land of Canaan,
